title: Port Scanner
desc: A TCP port scanner that probes a target host by attempting connections to a range of ports. Report each port as open (connection succeeded), closed (connection refused), or filtered (timeout). Support concurrent scanning with configurable thread count and timeout. Accept target host and port range via CLI args. Optionally detect service by banner grabbing after connection.

title: YouTube to MP3 Downloader
desc: Download audio from YouTube videos given a URL. Parse video pages to extract audio stream URLs, handle format selection (bitrate, codec), and save as MP3. Support playlist downloads and batch URL input from file. Handle rate limiting, retries, and progress reporting.

title: Imageboard (vichan-like)
desc: A web-based imageboard with thread-based forums. Users can create threads with text and image attachments, post replies, and see content in a catalog view. Implement board listing, thread creation with file uploads, reply display with image thumbnails, and basic moderation (delete posts, ban IPs).

title: URL Shortener Service
desc: A web service that accepts long URLs and returns shortened aliases. Store mappings in a database, redirect shortened URLs to original destinations. Support custom aliases, expiration dates, click tracking, and a REST API for programmatic access.

title: Pastebin / Code Snippet Service
desc: A web app for sharing text/code snippets with syntax highlighting. Accept paste content via web form or API, generate unique URLs, support expiration (burn-after-read, time-based), language detection, and raw text view.

title: IP/URL Obfuscation
desc: A tool that transforms IP addresses and URLs into alternative representations: hex, octal, integer, mixed-base, or Unicode homoglyphs. Accept an IP or URL as input, output all possible obfuscated forms. Optionally test that obfuscated forms resolve correctly via HTTP.

title: IP Tracking Geolocation Visualizer
desc: Resolve IP addresses to geographic locations and display them on a map. Use a GeoIP database (e.g. GeoLite2) to map IPs to lat/lng/city/country. Plot multiple IPs simultaneously, show connection paths between points, and display metadata (ISP, ASN, timezone).

title: Download Manager (multi-threaded, resume)
desc: A download manager that splits file downloads across multiple concurrent connections (HTTP Range headers) and supports resume on interruption. Show progress per-thread and overall, estimated time remaining, and speed. Handle redirects, authentication, and large files.

title: IRC Client
desc: An IRC protocol client that connects to IRC servers (RFC 1459/2812). Support multiple channels in tabs, nick completion, message history, CTCP, DCC file transfer, and server password authentication. Parse raw IRC messages and display formatted chat.

title: TCP/UDP Chat Server + Client
desc: A chat system with a central server and multiple clients using TCP for reliable messaging and UDP for optional features (presence pings, typing indicators). Support multiple chat rooms, private messaging, nicknames, and a text-based client UI.

title: Trivial File Transfer Protocol (TFTP)
desc: Implement TFTP (RFC 1350) server and client over UDP. Support read (RRQ) and write (WRQ) requests with fixed 512-byte data blocks, timeout-based retransmission, and error handling. Handle the lock-step acknowledgment protocol and duplicate packet detection.

title: Web Page Crawler
desc: A web crawler that starts from a seed URL, fetches pages, extracts links, and follows them within the same domain. Respect robots.txt, implement politeness delays between requests, avoid duplicate URLs, and output a site map or structured data.

title: Static Website Generator
desc: A tool that takes Markdown files with front-matter metadata and generates a complete static HTML site. Support templates/themes, auto-generated navigation, tag/category pages, RSS feeds, code syntax highlighting, and watch mode for live rebuild.

title: Stock Market Simulator (Yahoo finance data)
desc: A stock trading simulator that fetches historical or real-time data from Yahoo Finance API. Let users buy/sell stocks with virtual money, track portfolio value over time, show gains/losses, and plot price charts with moving averages.

title: Booru Image Board Downloader
desc: A CLI tool to batch download images from booru-style sites (e.g. Danbooru, Gelbooru) by tag search. Support pagination, concurrent downloads, tag filtering, file naming conventions, metadata export, and rate limit awareness.

title: HTTP/1.1 Web Server from Scratch
desc: A minimal HTTP/1.1 server that parses raw HTTP request lines and headers, serves static files from a document root, handles GET/HEAD/POST methods, returns appropriate status codes, supports keep-alive connections, and serves directory listings.

title: DNS Resolver (recursive from root)
desc: A recursive DNS resolver that starts at the root nameservers, follows delegations down to authoritative servers, and returns the final answer. Parse DNS wire-format messages, implement caching with TTL, handle CNAME chains, and support A, AAAA, MX, NS record types.

title: WebSocket Chat Server
desc: A real-time chat server using the WebSocket protocol (RFC 6455). Handle the HTTP upgrade handshake, frame encoding/decoding, broadcast messages to connected clients, support rooms, and include a simple web client.

title: Load Balancer (round-robin, health checks)
desc: A TCP/HTTP load balancer that distributes incoming connections across multiple backend servers. Implement round-robin and least-connections strategies. Periodically health-check backends (HTTP 200 or TCP connect) and remove unhealthy ones from rotation.

title: REST API Micro-Framework
desc: A lightweight REST framework with routing, request parsing, middleware support, and JSON response formatting. Support route parameters, query string parsing, request body validation, CORS headers, and error handling middleware.

title: GraphQL Server
desc: A GraphQL server that parses GraphQL query documents, validates them against a schema, and executes resolvers. Support queries, mutations, subscriptions, type definitions with arguments, and a GraphiQL-like explorer UI.

title: MQTT Broker (pub-sub messaging)
desc: An MQTT broker implementing the MQTT 3.1.1/5.0 protocol. Handle client connections, topic subscriptions with wildcards, QoS levels 0/1/2, retained messages, Last Will messages, and persistent sessions.

title: SOCKS5 Proxy Server
desc: A SOCKS5 proxy server (RFC 1928) that forwards TCP connections and UDP packets. Support the full handshake (auth methods negotiation, client request, address resolution), CONNECT command, UDP ASSOCIATE, and optional username/password authentication.

title: SMTP Mail Server
desc: An SMTP server that receives email via the SMTP protocol (RFC 5321), stores messages in a mailbox, and relays outgoing mail. Support EHLO, AUTH (PLAIN/LOGIN), MAIL FROM, RCPT TO, DATA commands. Handle SPF and DKIM verification.

title: OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect Provider
desc: An OAuth 2.0 authorization server with OpenID Connect extension. Support authorization code flow, implicit flow, client credentials flow, PKCE, token refresh, JWT-based ID tokens, and consent screens.

title: API Gateway (rate limiting, routing, auth)
desc: An API gateway that sits in front of microservices. Route requests based on path patterns, enforce rate limits (token bucket per client), validate API keys/JWT tokens, collect request/response metrics, and transform request/response headers.

title: Content Delivery Network (edge caching)
desc: A CDN edge proxy that caches static content at geographically distributed nodes. Implement cache eviction (LRU/TTL), stale-while-revalidate, cache-control header parsing, request coalescing, and origin pull with health-based origin selection.

title: Torrent Client (CLI or GUI)
desc: A BitTorrent client that parses .torrent files, connects to trackers (HTTP/UDP), discovers peers via DHT/PEX, downloads pieces in rarest-first order, verifies piece hashes, assembles the final file, and seeds back to the swarm.

title: Booru Client
desc: A desktop/GUI client for browsing booru image boards with advanced filtering, tag autocomplete, batch download, slideshow mode, and local tag management/personal tagging across multiple booru APIs.

title: Chan Aggregator (multi-board viewer)
desc: An aggregator that watches multiple imageboards across different chan sites, consolidates threads and replies into a unified feed with keyword alerts, thread watching, reply notifications, and content filtering.

title: Real-time Collaborative Editor (CRDT/OT)
desc: A collaborative text editor where multiple users edit the same document simultaneously. Implement Operational Transformation or CRDTs (e.g. Yjs) for conflict resolution. Use WebSockets for real-time sync with cursor position broadcasting.

title: Web Browser Rendering Engine (layout + CSS)
desc: A minimal browser engine that parses HTML and CSS, constructs a DOM tree, applies styles (cascade, specificity, inheritance), computes layout (block/inline, box model), and renders to a framebuffer or canvas. Support basic selectors and positioned elements.

title: Display MBR Contents
desc: Read the Master Boot Record from a disk device (e.g. /dev/sda), parse the partition table (MBR layout at offset 0x1BE), display partition entries (type, start sector, size, bootable flag), and optionally hex-dump the boot code area.

title: Elastic Producer/Consumer Task Queue
desc: A multi-threaded/process task queue where producers submit work and workers consume tasks dynamically. Support auto-scaling of workers based on queue depth, graceful shutdown, task acknowledgment, retry with backoff, and persistent queue storage.

title: Simple Version Control System
desc: A Git-like VCS with init, add, commit, log, diff, and branch operations. Store snapshots as content-addressed objects (blob, tree, commit) with SHA-1 hashes. Support branching, checkout, and a simple merge strategy (3-way merge).

title: Terminal Shell (pipes, redirection, history)
desc: A Unix shell that executes external commands with argument parsing, pipes (|), input/output redirection (>, <, >>), command history with up-arrow navigation, environment variable expansion, tab completion, and job control (&, fg, bg).

title: Virtual Machine (bytecode, Hello World)
desc: A stack-based VM with its own bytecode instruction set. Implement a core set of opcodes (push, pop, add, sub, jump, call, ret, load, store), a program counter, stack operations, and a simple assembler to compile textual assembly to bytecode.

title: Window Manager (tiling/floating, X11/Wayland)
desc: A window manager for X11 or Wayland that arranges windows in tiling or floating layouts. Support workspace switching, keyboard-driven window navigation, resize/move operations, window borders/title bars, and a configuration file for keybindings.

title: UART Game in Assembly
desc: A game (e.g. Snake, Pong) running on a microcontroller via UART serial communication. Implement the game logic in assembly, render frames as ASCII art sent over UART, and read input from the serial terminal (keyboard keys sent as bytes).

title: Resource Monitor (htop-like)
desc: A system resource monitor that displays CPU usage per-core, memory usage (RAM + swap), disk I/O, network throughput, and running processes. Use an interactive TUI (e.g. ncurses) with real-time updating columns, sortable by resource usage.

title: Packet Sniffer (tcpdump-like)
desc: A packet sniffer that captures network interfaces in promiscuous mode using raw sockets or pcap. Parse Ethernet, IPv4/IPv6, TCP, UDP, ICMP headers. Display source/dest IPs, ports, protocol, payload size. Support BPF filter expressions and output to pcap file.

title: System Call Tracer (strace-like)
desc: A system call tracer that uses ptrace to intercept syscalls made by a child process. Display syscall number, arguments (decode known structs), return value. Support filtering by syscall name, attach to running process, and show strace-style output.

title: Pong in UEFI (color graphics)
desc: Implement the game Pong running in UEFI with GOP (Graphics Output Protocol) for framebuffer access. Draw paddles, ball, and score using direct pixel manipulation. Handle UEFI timer events for game loop timing and keyboard input for controls.

title: FUSE Filesystem (mount custom FS)
desc: A FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) implementation that mounts a custom filesystem. Implement readdir, getattr, read, write, create, unlink, mkdir, rmdir operations. Back the filesystem with a simple flat file or custom on-disk format.

title: Memory Allocator (malloc/free)
desc: A dynamic memory allocator implementing malloc, free, calloc, realloc. Manage a heap region using a free list (implicit or explicit). Implement splitting and coalescing of free blocks. Handle alignment requirements and minimize fragmentation.

title: Linux Container from Scratch (namespaces + cgroups)
desc: A minimal Linux container runtime using kernel namespaces (PID, NET, MNT, UTS, USER) and cgroups v2 for resource limits. Spawn a child process in isolated namespaces with a chrooted filesystem. Set memory and CPU constraints via cgroupfs.

title: Docker-like Container Runtime
desc: A container runtime similar to Docker that pulls OCI images from a registry, unpacks layers into an overlayfs, configures networking with veth pairs and bridges, and runs the container with namespace isolation. Support exec, logs, stop, rm commands.

title: Device Driver (kernel module, chardev)
desc: A Linux kernel module that creates a character device (/dev/mydevice). Implement open, read, write, release file operations. Handle concurrency with mutexes. Accept arbitrary data from user-space, store it in a kernel buffer, and return on read.

title: Text Editor with Autosave (data in filename)
desc: A terminal text editor with file saving that encodes data into the filename itself (metadata in filename). Support basic editing (insert, delete, navigation), syntax highlighting via regex rules, search, undo/redo, and line numbers.

title: Bootloader (extended filesystem support)
desc: A bootloader that loads from disk, switches to protected mode (32-bit), sets up a minimal GDT, initializes the screen via VGA text mode, and loads a kernel from a FAT12/16/32 filesystem into memory and jumps to it. Read extended filesystem structures.

title: Operating System Kernel (x86_64, interrupts, paging)
desc: A minimal x86_64 kernel that boots in QEMU, sets up long mode with 4-level paging, configures GDT/IDT with interrupt handlers for keyboard and timer, implements a physical page allocator and a slab allocator, loads ELF binaries, handles syscalls (read/write/exit), implements round-robin process scheduling with process isolation via separate page tables, and boots to a shell that accepts keyboard input and runs minimal commands.

title: File System (ext2-like, directories, inodes)
desc: An ext2-like filesystem implemented as a disk image. Define superblock, block group descriptors, inode table, and data block bitmaps. Implement inode allocation, directory entries (create/delete/lookup files), block allocation with extents or block groups, and read/write file operations with proper inode metadata updates.

title: Process Scheduler (O(1) or CFS-like)
desc: An O(1) or CFS-like process scheduler. Maintain runqueues with priority arrays (active/expired for O(1)) or red-black tree (CFS). Implement context switching, timeslice calculation, priority adjustments, load balancing across CPUs, and sleep/wakeup queues.

title: Container Orchestrator (Kubernetes-lite)
desc: A minimal container orchestrator that manages deployment of containerized applications across a cluster. Implement pod/service abstractions, scheduling with resource constraints (CPU/mem), health checks (liveness/readiness probes), rolling updates, service discovery with DNS, and a CLI to manage deployments.

title: Init System (PID 1, service management)
desc: An init system (PID 1) that manages services as systemd units or rc scripts. Parse service definitions (dependencies, exec commands, restart policies), spawn services in parallel respecting dependency order, supervise running services with respawn on crash, handle SIGTERM/SIGCHLD, and provide a control tool (start/stop/restart/status).

title: Distributed Lock Service (etcd/Consul-like)
desc: A distributed lock service using the Raft consensus protocol for fault tolerance. Implement lock acquisition/release with TTL-based leases, lock renewal heartbeat, watch/lock change notifications, and a gRPC/REST API. Handle network partitions with quorum-based decisions and automatic leader election.

title: RAFT Consensus Algorithm
desc: The Raft consensus algorithm (Diego Ongaro, 2014). Implement leader election (request/response votes, randomized election timeouts), log replication (AppendEntries RPC with consistency check), safety (election restriction, committed entries), and persistence of log and snapshots. Expose a simple KV-store interface on top.

title: Password Manager (encrypted storage)
desc: A password manager that stores credentials encrypted at rest. Encrypt the vault using AES-256-GCM with a master password-derived key (PBKDF2/scrypt/Argon2). Support CRUD operations for entries (website, username, password), generate strong passwords, copy to clipboard with auto-clear, and unlock with master password.

title: LSB Steganography (hide data in images)
desc: Hide a secret message or file inside an image using LSB (Least Significant Bit) steganography. Modify the least significant bit of each RGB channel in each pixel to encode the secret data bit-by-bit. Support both encode and decode operations. Handle the image header correctly and optionally add encryption before embedding.

title: Matrix Arithmetic Library
desc: A matrix arithmetic library that supports creation, addition, subtraction, multiplication (naive and Strassen), transpose, determinant (cofactor expansion for small, LU decomposition for large), inverse (Gauss-Jordan), and solving Ax=b systems. Represent matrices as 2D arrays with generic numeric types.

title: File Compression Utility (Huffman/LZW)
desc: A file compression utility implementing Huffman coding or LZW. For Huffman: build frequency table, construct Huffman tree, assign variable-length codes, serialize tree+data. For LZW: build dictionary dynamically, encode repeated patterns. Support both compress and decompress with CLI input/output.

title: Bismuth Fractal Generator
desc: Generate Bismuth fractal images: a type of quartic fractal with colorful, ornate patterns. Implement the escape-time algorithm for the z = z^4 + c Julia-like iteration, map iteration counts to a color palette, and render at high resolution with smooth coloring and zoom capability.

title: Seam Carving (content-aware resize)
desc: Content-aware image resizing using seam carving. Compute an energy map (gradient magnitude), find the optimal vertical/horizontal seam (dynamic programming) that minimizes total energy, and remove/add seams to resize. Support both reduction and expansion, with optional mask for region preservation.

title: Bayesian Filter (spam classifier)
desc: A Bayesian spam filter that classifies emails as spam or ham using Naive Bayes. Tokenize message body, compute word probabilities from training corpus with Laplace smoothing, calculate combined probability using Bayes' theorem, and implement threshold-based classification with auto-update on user feedback.

title: CSV Parser (RFC 4180, with escaping)
desc: A CSV parser that conforms to RFC 4180. Handle quoted fields with embedded commas, newlines, and escaped quotes. Support custom delimiters, header row extraction, type detection (numeric, string), and output as structured data (list of dicts/objects). Handle malformed input gracefully with error reporting.

title: JSON Parser from Scratch
desc: A JSON parser that tokenizes and parses JSON according to ECMA-404. Implement a recursive descent parser for values, objects, strings, numbers, arrays, booleans, and null. Handle escaped characters in strings, Unicode escapes, scientific notation in numbers. Output a structured representation (map/list/primitives).

title: Bloom Filter (probabilistic set membership)
desc: A Bloom filter for probabilistic set membership. Use k hash functions (e.g. MurmurHash, FNV) over a bit array of size m. Support add() and contains() operations. Calculate false positive rate based on m, n, k. Optionally implement counting Bloom filter for deletions.

title: In-Memory KV Store (Redis-like, RDB/AOF)
desc: An in-memory key-value store similar to Redis with a TCP-based protocol. Support string, list, hash, set, sorted set data types with commands (GET/SET, LPUSH/LPOP, HSET/HGET, SADD, ZADD). Implement RDB snapshots (fork+write) and AOF append-only file persistence. Handle multiple concurrent clients.

title: B-Tree Implementation (disk-backed)
desc: A disk-backed B-Tree implementation. Support insert, search, delete with splitting and merging of nodes. Implement a page cache, buffer pool, and serialization of tree nodes to disk. Support variable-order trees with configurable page size. Expose cursor-based iteration for range queries.

title: Full-Text Search Engine (inverted index, TF-IDF)
desc: A full-text search engine with an inverted index. Tokenize documents with stemming and stop-word removal, build a postings list for each term with TF-IDF scores. Support boolean queries (AND, OR, NOT) and ranked search with cosine similarity. Index both documents and return ranked results with snippets.

title: Vector Embedding Service (cosine search)
desc: A vector embedding service that stores and searches high-dimensional vectors using cosine similarity. Support CRUD for vectors with metadata, search by nearest neighbor (exact or approximate), and vector arithmetic (add, subtract). Use a configurable distance metric (cosine, euclidean, dot).

title: Recommendation Engine (collaborative filtering)
desc: A recommendation engine using collaborative filtering. Implement user-based or item-based CF by computing similarity matrices (Pearson, cosine). Generate top-N recommendations for a user based on similar users' preferences. Support implicit feedback (purchases, clicks) and explicit ratings. Handle cold-start with popularity-based fallback.

title: Time Series Database (downsampling, retention)
desc: A time-series database optimized for timestamped data. Implement columnar storage with timestamp and value columns, downsampling/aggregation (avg, min, max, sum over time windows), retention policies (auto-delete old data), continuous queries for pre-aggregation, and HTTP query API with time range filters.

title: Graph Database (property graph, Cypher-like)
desc: A property graph database with nodes and directed edges that carry key-value properties. Implement a Cypher-like query language (MATCH, WHERE, RETURN, CREATE). Use an adjacency list or index-free adjacency for graph traversal. Support label-based filtering and property indexing.

title: Object Storage (S3-like: buckets, versioning)
desc: An S3-compatible object storage service. Support bucket CRUD, object upload/download with multipart upload, versioning with delete markers, bucket policies / presigned URLs, and listing with prefix/delimiter pagination. Store data on local filesystem with metadata in a database.

title: Data Pipeline / ETL Engine
desc: An ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipeline engine. Define pipelines as a DAG of operations (extract from CSV/JSON/API/database, transform with filter/map/aggregate/join, load to database/file). Support scheduled executions, incremental loads, error handling, and monitoring with logging.

title: Relational Database (SQL, relationships, indexes)
desc: A relational database with SQL support. Implement SQL parser (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, JOIN, WHERE, GROUP BY), B-tree indexes for fast lookups, query optimizer (join ordering, index selection), transaction support (ACID with WAL), and a client-server protocol.

title: SQLite from Scratch (SQL parser + B-Tree + executor)
desc: An SQLite-like embedded database from scratch. Implement a SQL tokenizer/parser, virtual machine that executes bytecode (VDBE), B-tree storage engine, pager with journal for atomic commits, and support for basic SQL operations (CREATE, INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE with WHERE).

title: LSM-Tree Storage Engine (LevelDB/RocksDB style)
desc: An LSM-Tree storage engine similar to LevelDB/RocksDB. Maintain in-memory memtables (sorted), flush to SSTable files on disk, implement compaction (leveled or size-tiered) to merge SSTables, support bloom filters for point lookups, and implement a merge iterator for range scans.

title: Columnar Storage Format (Parquet-like)
desc: A columnar storage format similar to Parquet or Arrow. Organize data in row groups with column chunks, use run-length encoding (RLE) and dictionary encoding for compression, store min/max/null statistics per column chunk for predicate pushdown, and support projection pushdown.

title: Distributed KV Store (Dynamo-style)
desc: A Dynamo-style distributed key-value store. Implement consistent hashing with virtual nodes for partition, sloppy quorum (R/W/N configurable), hinted handoff for availability during failures, vector clocks for version reconciliation, and gossip-based membership and failure detection.

title: CRDT Data Types (conflict-free replicated types)
desc: CRDT (Conflict-free Replicated Data Types) for collaborative systems. Implement G-Counter (grow-only counter), PN-Counter (positive/negative counters), G-Set/2P-Set (grow-only/two-phase set), LWW-Register (last-writer-wins register), and OR-Set (observed-remove set). Support merge operations that converge without coordination.

title: Simulate Individual Neurons (basic NN)
desc: Simulate individual neurons and basic neural computation. Model a neuron with weighted inputs, activation functions (sigmoid, tanh, ReLU), and threshold firing. Build a single-layer perceptron for binary classification (AND/OR/XOR). Visualize decision boundaries and learning via weight updates.

title: Curses Text Editor (Emacs/Vim keybindings)
desc: A terminal text editor with curses, supporting Emacs or Vim-style keybindings. Implement modal editing (normal, insert, visual modes for Vim), syntax highlighting, search with regex, copy/paste/delete operations, undo tree, line numbers, and configurable theme.

title: AI for Roguelikes (pathfinding, decisions)
desc: AI for roguelike games: implement A* pathfinding for enemy movement toward the player, state machines for enemy behavior (patrol, chase, attack, flee), simple decision trees for tactical choices (use item, heal, retreat), and line-of-sight/fov for enemy perception.

title: Sudoku/n-Puzzle Solver (A* algorithm)
desc: A solver for Sudoku puzzles or n-puzzles using the A* search algorithm. For Sudoku: use constraint propagation and backtracking with A* heuristic. For n-puzzle: A* with Manhattan distance or misplaced-tiles heuristic. Display the solving process step-by-step.

title: Connect-4 AI (Alpha-Beta pruning)
desc: A Connect Four AI using minimax with alpha-beta pruning. Implement the game board, valid move generation, win detection (horizontal, vertical, diagonal), evaluation function based on patterns (e.g. two/three in a row), and iterative deepening for time control.

title: Feed-Forward Neural Network (matrix-based, backprop)
desc: A feed-forward neural network from scratch using matrix operations. Implement forward propagation, backpropagation (gradient descent), activation functions (sigmoid, tanh, ReLU, softmax), loss functions (MSE, cross-entropy), and mini-batch training. Test on simple datasets like XOR or MNIST.

title: CNN for MNIST (using TensorFlow/Theano)
desc: A CNN for MNIST digit classification using TensorFlow or PyTorch. Build a LeNet-5 architecture: convolutional layers with ReLU, max pooling, fully connected layers, softmax output. Train on MNIST dataset, achieve >99% accuracy. Visualize filters and feature maps.

title: OpenAI Gym Reinforcement Learning Agent
desc: A reinforcement learning agent for an OpenAI Gym environment using DQN. Implement experience replay buffer, epsilon-greedy exploration, target network for stable Q-learning updates. Train on CartPole, LunarLander, or Atari games. Log and plot rewards over episodes.

title: CNN from Scratch (no frameworks, MNIST)
desc: A CNN implemented from scratch (no deep learning frameworks) for MNIST. Implement convolution (with multiple filters), max pooling, flatten, fully connected layers, softmax, cross-entropy loss, backpropagation through all layers. Train with mini-batch SGD. Achieve >95% accuracy.

title: Decision Tree / Random Forest (from scratch)
desc: A decision tree and random forest classifier from scratch. Implement ID3 or CART algorithm with entropy/Gini impurity for split selection, pruning for overfitting prevention, and majority voting for random forest with bootstrapped samples and random feature subsets.

title: Image Classifier (CIFAR-10 or similar)
desc: An image classifier using a CNN on CIFAR-10. Build and train a small CNN (conv+pool+fc layers) with data augmentation (random crop, flip, rotation). Plot training/validation curves, confusion matrix, and saliency maps. Achieve >80% test accuracy.

title: Anomaly Detection (time series / logs)
desc: An anomaly detection system for time-series data or log files. Implement statistical methods: z-score, moving average deviation, IQR-based outlier detection, and seasonal decomposition. For logs: parse structured logs, detect frequency anomalies and rare event patterns. Visualize anomalies on a timeline.

title: Face Detection (Viola-Jones or CNN)
desc: A face detection system using either the Viola-Jones cascade classifier (Haar-like features, AdaBoost, cascade of classifiers) or a CNN approach (SSD/YOLO-based). Draw bounding boxes around detected faces. Optimize for real-time detection on video streams.

title: Object Detection (YOLO-like)
desc: An object detection system similar to YOLO that detects and classifies multiple objects in an image. Divide image into grid cells, predict bounding boxes (x, y, w, h) with confidence scores and class probabilities for each cell. Use non-max suppression to eliminate duplicate detections.

title: Speech Recognition (audio to text)
desc: A speech recognition system that converts audio to text. Implement MFCC feature extraction (mel-frequency cepstral coefficients), a deep neural network (CNN+RNN or transformer) for acoustic modeling, and an n-gram or neural language model for decoding. Train on LibriSpeech or similar.

title: Text-to-Speech System
desc: A text-to-speech system that converts written text to spoken audio. Implement grapheme-to-phoneme conversion, prosody prediction (duration, pitch, energy), and a neural vocoder (WaveNet, WaveGlow, or LPCNet) to generate raw waveforms. Chain a Tacotron-like model for mel-spectrogram prediction.

title: Neural Machine Translation (seq2seq + attention)
desc: A neural machine translation system using sequence-to-sequence with attention. Implement an encoder-decoder RNN/LSTM architecture with Bahdanau or Luong attention, teacher forcing during training, and beam search decoding. Train on a parallel corpus (e.g. WMT datasets).

title: Reinforcement Learning Agent (DQN/PPO)
desc: A reinforcement learning agent implementing DQN or PPO. For DQN: deep Q-network with experience replay and target network. For PPO: clipped surrogate objective, advantage estimation (GAE), and multiple epochs of mini-batch updates. Train on continuous control (MuJoCo) or Atari environments.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) System
desc: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system. Index documents as embedding vectors in a vector database (Pinecone, Chroma, FAISS). For a user query, retrieve top-k relevant documents by cosine similarity, then feed them as context to an LLM (OpenAI API, Llama, Mistral) to generate an answer grounded in the retrieved text.

title: Recommendation System (matrix factorization)
desc: A recommendation system using matrix factorization (alternating least squares or SVD). Decompose the user-item interaction matrix into user and item latent factor matrices. Predict missing ratings by dot product. Support implicit feedback (weighted ALS). Evaluate with RMSE on a held-out test set.

title: Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)
desc: A Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) with a generator and discriminator trained adversarially. Implement alternating training: generator tries to fool discriminator, discriminator learns real vs fake. Train on MNIST or CIFAR-10. Generate samples, plot loss curves, and explore latent space interpolation.

title: Diffusion Model for Image Generation
desc: A diffusion model for image generation that learns to denoise random noise into coherent images. Implement the forward diffusion process (adding noise over T steps) and the reverse denoising process (predict added noise via a UNet). Train on a dataset and generate samples via ancestral sampling or DDIM.

title: Large Language Model (GPT-style transformer)
desc: A GPT-style transformer language model from scratch. Implement multi-head causal self-attention, positional encoding (learned or sinusoidal), feed-forward blocks with GELU, layer normalization, and residual connections. Train on text corpus (e.g. Shakespeare, Wikipedia) with next-token prediction objective.

title: Neural Network from First Principles (autograd engine)
desc: An autograd engine (automatic differentiation) from first principles. Build a computational graph where each operation records its inputs and gradient function. Implement forward pass to compute values, backward pass to compute gradients via the chain rule. Support scalar and tensor operations.

title: K-Means Clustering (unsupervised learning)
desc: K-Means clustering from scratch. Implement initialization (random or k-means++), assignment step (assign each point to nearest centroid by Euclidean distance), update step (recompute centroids as mean of assigned points), convergence check, and evaluation metrics (inertia, silhouette score).

title: Fizzbuzz (bonus: in assembly)
desc: A FizzBuzz program that prints numbers 1 to n, replacing multiples of 3 with Fizz, multiples of 5 with Buzz, and multiples of both with FizzBuzz. Optional: implement in x86_64/ARM assembly using syscall write to stdout and a loop with div/mod instructions.

title: RPN Calculator
desc: A Reverse Polish Notation (postfix) calculator. Accept expressions like '3 4 + 2 *' via CLI or interactive mode. Use a stack: push numbers, pop operands for operators (+, -, *, /, ^), push result. Support trigonometric and math functions (sin, cos, sqrt, log).

title: Count Character Occurrences (Unicode support)
desc: Count character/runes occurrences in a string with full Unicode support. Read input text, iterate over Unicode code points (not bytes), maintain a frequency map for each character, and print results sorted by frequency or code point. Handle multi-byte UTF-8 sequences correctly.

title: Tower of Hanoi
desc: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle for n disks. Implement recursively: move n-1 disks from source to auxiliary, move nth disk to target, move n-1 from auxiliary to target. Display the sequence of moves and optionally animate the state of three pegs after each move.

title: Markdown Parser (to HTML/ANSI)
desc: A Markdown parser that converts Markdown text to HTML or ANSI terminal output. Parse headings (#), bold/italic (*, _), links [text](url), images, code blocks (fenced with ```), lists (ordered and unordered), blockquotes (>), horizontal rules (---), and inline code.

title: Radix Base Converter
desc: A radix/base converter that converts numbers between bases 2 to 36. Accept input as string in source base, convert to integer, then output in target base. Handle fractional parts with configurable precision. Support negative numbers and common input formats (0x hex, 0b binary, 0o octal).

title: Write an Interpreted LISP-like Language
desc: An interpreter for a LISP-like language with S-expressions. Implement a reader/tokenizer, evaluator with lexical scoping, built-in functions (car, cdr, cons, define, lambda, if, eq, +, -, *, /), garbage collection, and a REPL. Support closures and higher-order functions.

title: HTML + JavaScript Debugger (breakpoints, step-through)
desc: An HTML+JavaScript debugger that sets breakpoints and steps through JS execution. Parse JS source to find breakable lines, instrument code with debugging hooks, display call stack and local variables on pause, and provide step-over/step-in/step-out and continue controls.

title: Template Engine (Mustache-like: vars, loops, conditionals)
desc: A template engine similar to Mustache that replaces {{variable}} placeholders with values, supports sections ({{#list}}...{{/list}} for loops and conditionals), partials ({{>partial}}), and comments ({{! comment }}). Compile templates to a function for fast repeated rendering.

title: CSS Parser (selectors, cascade, specificity)
desc: A CSS parser that reads stylesheets and builds a structured representation. Tokenize CSS into tokens (identifiers, strings, numbers, hashes, functions, delimiters), parse selectors (element, class, ID, attribute, pseudo-class, combinators), parse declarations (properties and values), compute specificity and cascade order.

title: Lexer / Tokenizer for a Programming Language
desc: A lexer/tokenizer for a programming language. Define token types (keywords, identifiers, numbers, strings, operators, delimiters). Implement a state machine or regex-based tokenizer that reads source code character by character, produces a stream of tokens with line/column positions, and handles whitespace and comments.

title: Parser Combinator Library
desc: A parser combinator library that allows building recursive descent parsers by combining small parsers. Implement basic combinators: sequence (and_then), choice (or), many, optional, map, between, separated_by. Define parsers for integers, strings, identifiers, and combine them to parse simple grammars.

title: Linter / Code Formatter (AST-based)
desc: An AST-based linter and code formatter. Parse source code into an AST, traverse the tree to find rule violations (unused variables, missing semicolons, incorrect indentation), and apply automatic fixes. Implement configurable rule sets and optionally format code according to a style guide.

title: REPL using a Compiled Language
desc: A REPL (Read-Eval-Print-Loop) for a compiled language. Create subprocess for compiler, watch source file changes, recompile and execute on input. Display compilation errors inline. Support multi-line input, command history, variable inspection, and integrate with a debugger.

title: Design an Esoteric Language
desc: Design and implement an esoteric programming language with a unique execution model (e.g. Brainfuck, Befunge, Whitespace). Define the language specification, implement an interpreter or compiler, create example programs, and optionally build an online playground or visual debugger.

title: Turing Machine Simulator
desc: A Turing machine simulator. Define a tape (infinite in both directions), a set of states, a transition function (current state, read symbol -> write symbol, move direction, next state). Load machine definitions from a file format. Simulate step-by-step with tape visualization and state highlighting.

title: LISP Interpreter (S-expressions, macros)
desc: A LISP interpreter that parses S-expressions, implements eval with lexical scoping (closures), supports define, lambda, if, cond, let, quote, car, cdr, cons, eq, atom, and arithmetic. Implement a garbage collector (mark-sweep or stop-and-copy). include a macro system (hygienic or defmacro-style).

title: Regex Engine (NFA/DFA-based)
desc: A regex engine implementing NFA/DFA-based pattern matching. Parse regex patterns (concatenation, alternation |, Kleene star *, +, ?, ., character classes [...], capturing groups) using Thompson's construction to build an NFA, optionally convert to DFA with subset construction. Execute the automaton for text matching.

title: Bytecode Interpreter (stack-based VM)
desc: A stack-based bytecode interpreter. Define an instruction set (push, pop, add, sub, mul, div, cmp, jmp, jz, call, ret, load, store, print). Read bytecode from a binary file, execute in a virtual machine loop with operand stack, call stack, and local variable array.

title: Package Manager (dependency resolution, build)
desc: A package manager that resolves dependencies and builds/installs packages. Parse package metadata files (name, version, dependencies). Implement dependency resolution (DFS with topological sort, conflict detection, version constraint satisfaction). Download packages from a registry, build from source, and install to target directory.

title: Unicode Converter (UTF-8, 16LE, 32LE, 32BE)
desc: A Unicode converter that converts between UTF-8, UTF-16LE, UTF-16BE, UTF-32LE, and UTF-32BE. Implement encoding detection via BOM (Byte Order Mark), encode/decode logic for each encoding (handling surrogate pairs for UTF-16, leading byte sequences for UTF-8), and support for invalid sequence handling.

title: C Compiler (to x86_64 or ARM assembly)
desc: A C compiler that compiles a subset of C to x86_64 or ARM assembly. Implement lexer, parser (recursive descent or yacc), semantic analysis (type checking, symbol table), intermediate representation (IR or AST), code generation for arithmetic, control flow, function calls, pointers, arrays, and structs.

title: Garbage Collector (mark-sweep or copying)
desc: A garbage collector implementing mark-sweep or copying (stop-and-copy) collection. For mark-sweep: traverse root set, mark reachable objects, sweep unmarked objects and return memory to free list. For copying: divide heap into from-space and to-space, copy live objects during collection. Handle interior pointers and finalizers.

title: TypeScript-to-JS Transpiler (type checking, emit)
desc: A TypeScript-like to JavaScript transpiler that strips type annotations and performs basic type checking. Parse TS syntax (type annotations, interfaces, enums, generics), resolve type errors, emit JS code. Handle module imports/exports, arrow functions, classes, async/await.

title: JIT Compiler (x86 code generation at runtime)
desc: A JIT compiler that generates x86_64 machine code at runtime. Define an IR, implement code generation for arithmetic, control flow, and function calls. Use mmap with PROT_EXEC to allocate executable memory, jump to generated code. Implement a simple method-based compilation cache.

title: WebAssembly Runtime (load, validate, execute .wasm)
desc: A WebAssembly runtime that loads .wasm modules, validates the module structure (magic number, sections, type checking), and executes instructions. Implement the WASM stack machine (control flow, memory, function calls), linear memory with bounds checking, and import/export resolution.

title: Image to ASCII Art
desc: Convert an image to ASCII art. Load the image, resize to target dimensions while preserving aspect ratio, convert to grayscale, map each pixel's brightness to an ASCII character from a ramp (e.g. @%#*+=-:. ), and output as text. Optionally preserve color using ANSI escape codes.

title: HSV Color Representation (color picker)
desc: An HSV color picker tool. Let users select hue (0-360), saturation (0-100%), and value/brightness (0-100%) via sliders. Show the resulting color in real-time. Implement HSV to RGB conversion and display the color swatch. Show complementary and analogous color harmonies.

title: Generate Complimentary Color
desc: A tool that generates the complementary (opposite on the color wheel), analogous (adjacent), triadic, and tetradic color schemes for any given color. Compute these using HSL hue rotation. Display the scheme as colored swatches with hex values and a preview of the combination.

title: Spinning 3D Cube (software-rendered)
desc: A software-rendered 3D cube that rotates continuously. Define 8 vertices in 3D space, apply rotation matrices (x, y, z axes), project to 2D using perspective projection, draw edges between projected points using line drawing (Bresenham). Optionally fill faces with shading.

title: Cellular Textures (automata-based)
desc: Generate textures using cellular automata rules. Implement a 2D grid of cells, apply rules (e.g. Conway's Game of Life, WireWorld, rule 30 in 2D, or cyclic automata) iteratively to generate patterns. Map cell states to colors. Support random seeding and interactive editing.

title: Old School Demo Effect (plasma, tunnel, scrollers)
desc: Old-school demo effects: plasma (sum of sine waves over pixel coordinates), tunnel (polar coordinate texture mapping with distance-based distortion), and text scrollers (horizontal or vertical text scrolling with color cycling). Render real-time with pixel-level manipulation.

title: Sierpinski Triangle (recursive/chaos game)
desc: Generate a Sierpinski triangle using recursion (subdividing a triangle into 3 smaller triangles) or the chaos game (randomly select a vertex, move halfway toward it, plot point). Implement both methods and animate the construction process. Support configurable depth/iterations.

title: Ulam Spiral (prime number visualization)
desc: Visualize the Ulam spiral: arrange integers in a square spiral, highlight prime numbers. Generate the spiral grid, test each integer for primality (trial division or sieve), color prime cells differently. Support zoom, pan, and display of diagonal patterns in prime distribution.

title: ASCII Digital Clock
desc: A digital clock displayed in large ASCII characters. Define 7-segment or custom ASCII representations for digits 0-9 with colon separator. Update every second. Support 12h/24h format, show current system time, and optionally display date.

title: Pixel Editor (draw, save to image format)
desc: A pixel editor/graphics program with a grid of pixels, color picker, paintbrush, eraser, line, rectangle, and circle tools. Support undo/redo, save/load images (BMP/PNG), zoom in/out with scroll, and adjustable brush size. Use canvas-like widget or framebuffer.

title: Music Visualizer (bars, waveforms)
desc: A real-time music visualizer that displays audio as bars (frequency spectrum via FFT) and waveforms (time-domain amplitude). Use an audio capture API (microphone or system audio) or read from a file. Implement FFT to get frequency bins, map amplitudes to bar heights with averaging and decay.

title: Generate 5-Color Scheme from Image
desc: Extract a 5-color palette from an image. Use k-means clustering (k=5) on RGB pixels, or median cut quantization. Display the palette as color swatches with hex values. Optionally let the user export the palette to Adobe Swatch Exchange (ASE) or CSS variables.

title: 64KB Audio/Visual Demo
desc: A 64KB audio/visual demo that fits entirely in 64KB executable. Use procedural generation for both visuals (raymarching, particle systems, fractals, mandelbrot zoom) and audio (synthesized music/drums via mathematical functions). Use assembly-level optimization and data compression.

title: Image Converter (format conversion)
desc: An image converter that reads images in one format (PNG, JPEG, BMP, GIF, TIFF) and outputs in another. Use a library (e.g. stb_image, ImageMagick, or PIL) or implement decoders/encoders for simple formats. Support resizing, quality setting, and batch conversion by glob pattern.

title: Fractal Explorer (Mandelbrot/Julia zoom)
desc: An interactive fractal explorer for the Mandelbrot and Julia sets. Render using escape-time algorithm with smooth coloring (normalized iteration count). Support zoom (click to zoom in, scroll wheel), pan, and adjustable max iterations. Show Julia set for a selected point in the Mandelbrot set.

title: SVG Renderer (parse and render paths)
desc: An SVG renderer that parses SVG files (XML-based vector graphics) and renders them to a canvas or bitmap. Support basic shapes (rect, circle, ellipse, line, polyline, polygon, path), transforms (translate, rotate, scale), stroke and fill with colors, and basic CSS styling.

title: Particle System (interactive physics)
desc: An interactive particle system with physics simulation. Particles have position, velocity, acceleration, mass, and lifetime. Apply forces: gravity, wind, attraction/repulsion from mouse clicks. Implement collision with boundaries and elastic bouncing. Use a spatial hash grid for efficient particle-particle interactions.

title: GIF Creator/Editor (create from frames)
desc: A GIF creator/editor that assembles animated GIFs from individual image frames. Load frames, set frame delay, configure loop count, optimize with transparent color and frame differencing. Optionally extract frames from existing GIFs and reorder/delete frames.

title: Double Pendulum Simulation (Lagrangian mechanics)
desc: Simulate a double pendulum using Lagrangian mechanics. Derive equations of motion (four first-order ODEs), integrate using Runge-Kutta 4th order (RK4). Render the two pendulums as connected rods with a trace of the endpoint path. Show chaotic behavior with slight initial condition changes.

title: Constructive Solid Geometry (boolean 3D ops)
desc: Constructive Solid Geometry (CSG) library that performs boolean operations on 3D solids: union, intersection, subtraction. Represent solids as meshes, implement the BSP-tree-based boolean algorithm to compute new meshes. Render the resulting shapes or export as STL/OBJ.

title: N-Body Simulation (gravitational, particle merging)
desc: An N-body gravitational simulation. Each body exerts gravitational force on all others (F = G*m1*m2/r^2). Use the Barnes-Hut algorithm (quadtree for 2D, octree for 3D) to approximate far-field forces O(n log n) instead of O(n^2). Support particle merging on collision. Render as points or spheres.

title: Ray Tracer (spheres, lighting, shadows, reflections)
desc: A ray tracer that renders 3D scenes with spheres and planes. Implement primary ray generation, ray-sphere and ray-plane intersection, Phong/Blinn-Phong shading (ambient, diffuse, specular), shadows (shadow rays toward light sources), reflections (recursive ray tracing), and optionally refraction and antialiasing.

title: Real-time FFT Spectrum Visualizer
desc: A real-time audio spectrum visualizer using FFT. Capture audio input (microphone or line-in), apply window function (Hamming/Hann), compute FFT, map frequency bins to visual elements (bars, circular spectrum, waterfall display). Update at display refresh rate with smooth interpolation between frames.

title: 3D Software Renderer (rasterization pipeline)
desc: A 3D software renderer implementing the rasterization pipeline. Transform vertices (model, view, projection matrices), clip triangles, rasterize to pixels with z-buffer for hidden surface removal, apply per-pixel lighting (Gouraud or Phong shading), and texture mapping with bilinear filtering.

title: Voxel Engine (Minecraft-like block rendering)
desc: A voxel engine similar to Minecraft. Store the world as a 3D array of voxel blocks (chunked). Use meshing (greedy or culling invisible faces) to reduce triangles. Render with OpenGL or software. Support block placement/removal, simple lighting/sunlight propagation, and basic terrain generation (Perlin noise).

title: PDF Renderer (parse and render documents)
desc: A PDF renderer that parses and renders PDF documents. Parse the cross-reference table, object streams, page tree, content streams (operators for text, paths, images). Implement PDF text encoding (CMap, ToUnicode), vector path rendering, image decompression (Flate, DCT), and transparency groups.

title: Rock Paper Scissors
desc: A Rock Paper Scissors game. Accept player choice (r/p/s or rock/paper/scissors via input), generate computer choice randomly, determine winner by rules: rock beats scissors, scissors beats paper, paper beats rock. Keep score over multiple rounds. Play again prompt.

title: Simon (memory pattern game)
desc: Simon memory game: a sequence of colors/tones is shown, player repeats it. Each round adds one more step. Generate random sequence, display using colored buttons or console output (R G B Y), read player input, check correctness, increase sequence length on success, game over on wrong input.

title: Snake
desc: Snake game: control a snake that moves continuously in 4 directions. The snake grows when it eats food. Game over when it hits the wall or itself. Implement with a grid-based coordinate system, keyboard input (arrow keys), score tracking, and increasing speed as the snake grows.

title: Connect Four
desc: Connect Four: two-player game on a 7x6 grid. Players drop colored pieces into columns. Pieces fall to the lowest empty row. Win by connecting four pieces horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. Detect win/tie conditions, validate column fills, switch turns.

title: Yahtzee
desc: Yahtzee dice game. Roll 5 dice, select which to keep, reroll up to 3 times. Score according to categories (ones through sixes, three/four of a kind, full house, small/large straight, Yahtzee, chance). Track scoresheet with 13 rounds. Computer opponent optional.

title: Oil Panic
desc: Oil Panic (Donkey Kong-style barrel throwing/stacking game). Debris/barrels fall down tiers, player catches/redirects them. Implement tiered platforms, collision detection, falling object physics, simple scoring. Use keyboard input for movement.

title: Game Engine in Unity
desc: A simple game prototype in Unity engine. Set up a scene with basic physics (rigid bodies, colliders), player movement (WASD/arrow keys), camera following, and goal/obstacle objects. Demonstrate prefab usage, scene management, and Unity scripting (C#) with Update/FixedUpdate.

title: PDF Tagger
desc: PDF Tagger: a tool that reads PDF files and adds/edits metadata tags (title, author, subject, keywords). Parse PDF document info dictionary, modify /Info entries, support XMP metadata extraction and injection. List metadata of multiple PDFs in batch.

title: Flappy Bird Clone
desc: Flappy Bird clone: a side-scrolling game where a bird flaps (space/tap to apply upward velocity) to navigate through gaps between pipes. Implement physics (gravity, velocity), pipe generation with random gap heights, collision detection (rect vs rect), score counter, and game over on collision.

title: Knight's Tour
desc: Knight's Tour: find a sequence of moves of a knight on a chessboard such that the knight visits every square exactly once. Implement Warnsdorff's heuristic (choose the square with fewest onward moves) for efficient solving. Visualize the path on a chessboard with step numbers.

title: Tetris
desc: Tetris: falling tetrominoes (7 shapes: I, O, T, S, Z, J, L). Implement piece rotation with wall kicks, gravity (piece falls at increasing speed), line clearing with scoring (1-4 lines), next-piece preview, hold piece, hard drop, and level progression. Use grid-based board with collision detection.

title: Pipe Dreams
desc: Pipe Dreams / Pipe Mania puzzle game. A grid of pipe tiles is shown, and fluid flows from a source. Player rotates and places pipe pieces to connect the source to the drain before fluid reaches the end. Implement fluid propagation through connected pipes, level editor, and timer.

title: Pac-Man (different ghost behaviors)
desc: Pac-Man: control Pac-Man through a maze, eat pellets and avoid ghosts. Implement the maze as a tile grid, pathfinding for ghosts (targeting modes: chase, scatter, frightened, using distance to Pac-Man or ambush), power pellets that let Pac-Man eat ghosts, fruit bonuses, level progression.

title: Minesweeper
desc: Minesweeper: grid of cells, some conceal mines. Left-click reveals a cell: if mine, game over; otherwise show number of adjacent mines. If zero, auto-reveal neighboring cells (flood fill). Right-click flags suspected mines. First click always safe. Win by revealing all non-mine cells.

title: Sudoku (generator + solver)
desc: Sudoku generator and solver. Generator: start with a complete valid grid (backtracking or pattern-based), remove cells while maintaining unique solution. Solver: backtracking algorithm with constraint propagation (naked singles, hidden singles). Support 9x9 grid with 3x3 subgrids. Show steps.

title: Shuffle a Deck of Cards (visualization)
desc: Visualize deck of cards shuffling algorithms: Fisher-Yates (Knuth) shuffle, riffle shuffle, overhand shuffle. Display cards as colored ASCII or graphical representations. Show the deck before/after shuffling. Run multiple shuffles and measure randomness (e.g. correlation between adjacent cards).

title: Multi-Agent Tag Simulation
desc: Multi-agent tag simulation: agents move in a bounded 2D space, one is 'it' and must tag others. When tagged, that agent becomes 'it'. Implement simple pursuit-evasion AI, visualization of agents with trails, configurable speed and number of agents. Record tag counts and chase durations.

title: Scorched Earth Clone (artillery, wind, terrain)
desc: Scorched Earth-like artillery game. Two tanks positioned on a destructible terrain (height map). Players enter angle and power to fire a projectile. Simulate projectile motion with gravity, wind, and terrain collision. On impact, deform the terrain (remove pixels). Turn-based gameplay.

title: Chess (full rules)
desc: Chess with full rules. Implement a board representation (8x8 grid), piece movement (pawn, rook, knight, bishop, queen, king) with legal move generation, castling, en passant, pawn promotion, check/checkmate/stalemate detection, move validation, and a simple AI (minimax with alpha-beta pruning and evaluation function).

title: Mastermind
desc: Mastermind code-breaking game. Codemaker sets a 4-color secret code (6 colors, duplicates allowed). Codebreaker guesses, codemaker gives feedback: correct color and position (black pegs), correct color wrong position (white pegs). Implement both human as codemaker and human as codebreaker modes with a solving AI (Donald Knuth's algorithm).

title: Missile Command
desc: Missile Command: player defends cities from incoming missiles by launching interceptor missiles. Missiles explode at target point and destroy enemy missiles in a radius. Implement multiple missile silos with limited ammo that recharges, increasing difficulty with more/faster missiles, score tracking.

title: Tron Light Cycle
desc: Tron Light Cycle game: two players leave trails as they move on a grid. A player loses if they hit a wall, their own trail, or the opponent's trail. Implement 4-directional movement, increasing speed, collision detection, and optional AI opponent. Play on a single keyboard (WASD vs arrows).

title: Breakout
desc: Breakout: paddle at bottom controls left/right movement, ball bounces off paddle, walls, and bricks. Each brick breaks on hit, score increases. Implement paddle collision angle affecting ball direction, multiple brick types (tough bricks require multiple hits), power-ups (wide paddle, multi-ball), lives system.

title: Platformer Game Engine (tiles, gravity, enemies)
desc: A platformer game engine with tile-based levels, gravity, jumping, collision detection with tiles, moving platforms, enemy AI (patrol, chase, simple attack), collectible items, checkpoints, and a level editor. Render sprites for player, enemies, tiles. Implement camera following.

title: Monster Raising/Breeding Simulator
desc: A monster raising/breeding simulator. Monsters have stats (HP, ATK, DEF, SPEED), types, and evolution trees. Two monsters can breed to produce offspring with inherited/mutated stats. Implement battle system (turn-based), feeding/care mechanics, and a UI for managing your monster collection.

title: RPG Engine (Dragon Quest-like)
desc: A Dragon Quest-like RPG engine. Feature turn-based combat with party of characters vs enemies, leveling system (XP, stat growth), equipment (weapons, armor, accessories), inventory management, tile-based world map with towns and dungeons, NPC dialogue, quest tracking, and save/load game state to file.

title: First-Person Engine (OpenGL, walking, looking)
desc: A first-person 3D engine using OpenGL. Implement camera movement (WASD + mouse look), loading and rendering 3D models (OBJ format), texture mapping, lighting (directional, point, with Phong shading), collision detection with walls/floors, and a simple scene with objects to interact with.

title: Wolfenstein 3D Clone (ray-cast FPS)
desc: Wolfenstein 3D-style ray-casting engine. Cast rays from the player's position for each vertical screen strip, detect wall intersections using DDA (Digital Differential Analyzer) on a 2D grid map, render textured wall strips with distance-based scaling, support textured floor/ceiling, and sprite rendering (enemies, items) with billboarding.

title: Danmaku (Bullet Hell) Engine
desc: A bullet hell (danmaku) engine with complex pattern generation. Player ship moves in all directions, enemies fire dense bullet patterns (circular sprays, spirals, aimed shots). Implement collision detection (hitbox smaller than sprite), bullet pool for performance, screen shake on hit, boss fight with multiple pattern phases.

title: Roguelike Engine / Dungeon Generator
desc: A roguelike engine with procedural dungeon generation (BSP or cellular automata for rooms/corridors), turn-based movement, FOV (field of view using raycasting or shadowcasting), inventory, combat (melee/ranged), monster AI (wander, chase, attack), item placement, experience/leveling, and permadeath.

title: Go (board game, no AI needed)
desc: Go board game: 19x19 grid, players place stones alternately. Implement stone capture (when a group has no liberties), ko rule, scoring (territory + captured stones). Support handicap stones, komi, and SGF file save/load. Optionally implement a basic Go AI using Monte Carlo tree search.

title: Physics Engine (rigid bodies, collision, constraints)
desc: A physics engine for rigid bodies. Handle collision detection (SAT for convex polygons, circle-circle, AABB), collision response (impulse-based resolution with friction and restitution), constraints (distance joints, pendulums), broad-phase (spatial grid or sweep-and-prune) for efficiency, and sleeping for static objects.

title: Chip-8 Emulator (cached interpreter + games)
desc: A Chip-8 emulator that loads ROMs and runs them. Implement the Chip-8 CPU (35 opcodes, registers, program counter, stack, timers), memory (4KB RAM, font sprites), keyboard input (hex keypad mapped to keyboard), and display (64x32 monochrome, drawn via sprites XORed on screen). Include a few classic ROMs.

title: ROT 13
desc: ROT13 cipher: shift each letter by 13 positions (A->N, B->O, etc.). Apply twice to get original. Preserve case for letters, leave non-letters unchanged. Implement as a CLI filter: reads stdin, writes ROT13 output to stdout. Optionally support ROT5 (digits), ROT47 (all printable ASCII).

title: Caesar Cipher Cracker (brute-force + freq analysis)
desc: Caesar cipher cracker that tries all 25 shifts, outputs each result. Use frequency analysis (compare letter distribution to English letter frequencies using chi-squared statistic) to rank the most likely shift. Display top 3 candidate decryptions with confidence scores.

title: Password Generator (configurable options)
desc: A configurable password generator. Options: length, include uppercase, lowercase, digits, special characters, exclude ambiguous characters (Il1O0). Generate using a CSPRNG (secrets module or /dev/urandom). Ensure at least one character from each selected category. Support passphrase generation (XKCD-style) from a word list.

title: Classical Cipher Implementation (from rumkin.com)
desc: Implement a collection of classical ciphers from rumkin.com: Atbash (reverse alphabet), Vigenere, Playfair (5x5 grid digraph substitution), Autokey, Beaufort, Porta, and Running Key. Each cipher supports both encryption and decryption. Provide a unified CLI with cipher name as argument.

title: Text to Hex/Binary Converter
desc: A text to hex/binary converter. Accept input text, output each character as its hexadecimal (0xNN) and binary (NNNNNNNN) representation. Support both directions: hex/binary to text. Handle UTF-8 encoding properly (multi-byte sequences). Optionally format as columns or space-separated bytes.

title: Vigenere Cipher (encryption/decryption)
desc: Vigenere cipher encryption and decryption. Use a keyword to generate a repeating key, shift each plaintext letter by the key letter's position (A=0). Support both standard mode (shifts forward) and Beaufort variant (shifts backward). Preserve case and non-alphabetic characters.

title: Encrypt File and Upload Online
desc: Encrypt a file (using AES-256-GCM or similar) and upload it to an online service (SCP, S3, or a custom server). CLI: file path, destination. Generate a random key, encrypt with authenticated encryption, upload the ciphertext. Print decryption instructions. Optionally split key into shares (Shamir).

title: ID3 Reader (read/edit MP3 metadata tags)
desc: An ID3 tag reader for MP3 files. Parse ID3v1 (fixed 128-byte footer: title, artist, album, year, comment, genre) and ID3v2 (variable-length header at file start with frames: TIT2, TPE1, TALB, etc.). Display tag information. Support editing and writing tags back to the file.

title: Hash Function (SHA-256 from scratch)
desc: SHA-256 hash function implemented from scratch. Process message in 512-bit blocks, expand message schedule, iterate 64 compression rounds with constant K values. Implement padding: append 0x80, zeros, 64-bit length. Test against known vectors (e.g. 'abc' -> ba7816bf8f01cfea...). Support hashing files and strings.

title: AES Encryption (AES-128/256 from scratch)
desc: AES encryption (AES-128 and AES-256) from scratch. Implement SubBytes (S-box lookup), ShiftRows (row rotation), MixColumns (GF(2^8) multiplication), AddRoundKey (XOR). Key expansion for round keys. Support ECB and CBC modes with PKCS#7 padding. Test against NIST test vectors.

title: TOTP / 2FA App (Google Authenticator-like)
desc: A TOTP/2FA authenticator app similar to Google Authenticator. Generate time-based one-time passwords (RFC 6238): HMAC-SHA1 with time step (30s), truncate to 6-8 digits. Support HOTP (counter-based, RFC 4226). Scan QR codes or manually enter secret keys. Display rotating codes with countdown timer.

title: JWT Library (encode, decode, verify)
desc: A JWT library for encoding, decoding, and verifying JSON Web Tokens (RFC 7519). Support signing algorithms: HMAC (HS256), RSA (RS256), ECDSA (ES256). Parse JWT into header, payload, signature. Verify signature. Generate tokens with custom claims and expiration. Handle base64url encoding correctly.

title: Markdown (HTML/XML) Editor
desc: A Markdown/HTML/XML editor with live preview. Split pane: edit raw markup on the left, rendered output on the right. Update preview in real-time as user types. Support syntax highlighting in the editor pane. Export rendered output as HTML or PDF.

title: RSA from Scratch (keygen, encrypt, sign)
desc: RSA implementation from scratch: key generation (generate large primes p, q, compute n=pq, phi(n), choose e, compute d with extended Euclidean algorithm), encryption/decryption (modular exponentiation with square-and-multiply), signing/verification. Support at least 2048-bit keys. Test against known vectors.

title: Certificate Authority (issue X.509 certs)
desc: A simple Certificate Authority that issues X.509 certificates. Generate a CA root certificate (self-signed), process CSR (Certificate Signing Request) files, sign them to produce certificates with validity periods, subject info, and extensions (SAN, key usage). Verify certificate chains.

title: Encrypted Messaging (Signal-like, E2E encryption)
desc: An end-to-end encrypted messaging system similar to Signal. Implement the X3DH (Extended Triple Diffie-Hellman) key agreement protocol for initial key exchange, Double Ratchet algorithm for forward secrecy and post-compromise security, and message encryption with AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305.

title: Key Press Bot / Automation Tool
desc: A key press bot / automation tool that simulates keyboard input. Record sequences of key presses with timing, replay them on demand. Support configurable loops, delays between keystrokes, and mouse automation (click, move, drag). Use platform-specific input simulation APIs (X11, WinAPI, CGEvent).

title: TLS 1.3 Handshake (minimal implementation)
desc: A minimal TLS 1.3 handshake implementation. Implement the ClientHello/ServerHello exchange, key exchange using (EC)DHE, cipher suite negotiation (AES-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305), certificate verification, Finished message authentication, and record layer encryption. Support resumption with PSK.

title: VPN Server (TUN/TAP, packet encryption)
desc: A VPN server using TUN/TAP devices. Create a TUN interface, assign IP, encrypt/decrypt IP packets between client and server. Implement a simple protocol: packet encapsulation with encryption (AES-GCM), key exchange, and framing. Route client traffic through the tunnel. Support multiple connected clients.

title: Tor-like Anonymity Network (onion routing)
desc: A Tor-like anonymity network with onion routing. Implement a directory service for node discovery, build circuits through multiple onion routing nodes with layered encryption (ephemeral Diffie-Hellman key exchange per hop), relay cells through the circuit, and support hidden services (.onion addresses).

title: Calculate First n Digits of Pi (BBP/Machin)
desc: Calculate the first n digits of Pi using the Bailey-Borwein-Plouffe (BBP) formula (hexadecimal digits directly) or Machin-like formula (arctan series). Implement arbitrary-precision arithmetic for the series computation. Output digits to console or file. Support n > 1000 digits.

title: Highest Prime Factor Calculator
desc: Find the highest prime factor of a given integer. Use trial division up to sqrt(n) for small factors, then Pollard's Rho algorithm for large factors. Handle edge cases: prime input, perfect squares, large numbers (>10^12). Output all prime factors sorted, with multiplicity.

title: Dot and Cross Product of Vectors
desc: Compute dot product and cross product of 3D vectors. Dot: sum of component-wise products (scalar). Cross: (a2*b3 - a3*b2, a3*b1 - a1*b3, a1*b2 - a2*b1) as vector. Support n-dimensional dot product. Show both numeric inputs and result. Include magnitude and angle between vectors.

title: Eulerian Path (use every edge once)
desc: Find an Eulerian path (uses every edge exactly once) in a graph. Implement Hierholzer's algorithm: find a starting node, follow edges until returning to start, then splice in cycles from unused edges. Support directed and undirected graphs. Verify Eulerian circuit vs path conditions.

title: Stock Max Profit (optimal buy/sell window)
desc: Find the maximum profit from a single buy and sell of a stock given daily prices. Find the optimal buy day (lowest valley) and sell day (highest peak after buy) in O(n) time. Output buy price, sell price, profit. Support multiple transactions with optional cooldown period.

title: Random Name Picker (weighted selection)
desc: A weighted random name picker/selector. Accept a list of names with optional weights (frequency/probability). Implement weighted random selection: prefix sum of weights, binary search for random value. Support without-replacement sampling (remove after selection). Display selected name with animation.

title: Least Squares Fitting (linear regression)
desc: Least squares linear regression: given (x, y) points, compute slope m and intercept b such that y = mx + b minimizes sum of squared residuals. Calculate R-squared, standard error, and residuals. Plot the points and fitted line. Optionally support polynomial regression.

title: Game of Life (cellular automaton, pattern editor)
desc: Conway's Game of Life cellular automaton with a pattern editor. Implement the grid, rules (birth on 3 neighbors, survival on 2-3, death otherwise), generation stepping (paused or continuous). Include common patterns (glider, blinker, toad, beacon, glider gun). Editor: click to toggle cells, load/save patterns as RLE format.

title: Dijkstra's Algorithm (shortest path)
desc: Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm on a weighted graph. Maintain a priority queue of nodes by distance, relax edges. Support directed/undirected graphs. Output shortest distances and path from source to each node. Visualize the graph and step-by-step exploration.

title: First 1k Digits of Pi (spigot algorithm)
desc: Compute the first 1000 digits of Pi using a spigot algorithm (Rabinowitz-Wagon). The algorithm produces digits sequentially without storing previous results. Implement using integer arithmetic with arrays for the computation. Output groups of 10 digits per line for readability.

title: Mandelbrot Set (escape-time fractal)
desc: Render the Mandelbrot set using the escape-time algorithm. For each pixel, iterate z = z^2 + c until |z| > 2 or max iterations. Color based on iteration count (smooth coloring with normalized iteration count). Support zoom and pan with re-center on click.

title: Bellman-Ford Simulation (5+ vertices)
desc: Simulate the Bellman-Ford algorithm on a graph with 5+ vertices and detect negative cycles. Initialize distances to infinity, set source to 0, relax all edges V-1 times, then check for negative cycles. Display step-by-step distance updates on a visual graph.

title: Nonogram Generator + Solver
desc: A Nonogram (Picross) generator and solver. Generator: fill a grid, compute row/col clues (consecutive block lengths). Solver: use line solving (mark cells as filled/crossed based on clue constraints) and backtracking for ambiguous cases. Both grid and clue display.

title: A* Pathfinding (heuristic search, grid)
desc: A* pathfinding algorithm on a 2D grid with obstacles. Implement open/closed sets, heuristic (Manhattan, Euclidean, Chebyshev), cost function g (movement cost, e.g. 1 for cardinal, 1.414 for diagonal). Show explored nodes and final path. Support wall toggling and weighted cells.

title: C++ IDE Plugin (Clang AST autocomplete for Sublime/Atom)
desc: A C++ IDE plugin for Sublime Text or Atom that provides Clang AST-based autocompletion. Use libclang to parse C++ code, extract completions at cursor position (types, functions, members). Display suggestions in a popup with documentation. Update as user types.

title: Bloom Filter (probabilistic membership)
desc: A Bloom filter (probabilistic set membership) with configurable false positive rate. Use k hash functions (MurmurHash, FNV-1a, DJB2) over a bit array. Implement add() and mightContain(). Calculate optimal k and m from expected n and false positive rate p = (1 - e^(-kn/m))^k.

title: Quadtree / Octree (spatial partitioning)
desc: A Quadtree (2D) or Octree (3D) for spatial partitioning. Insert points/objects into tree nodes, split nodes when capacity exceeded. Support range/region queries (find all points within a rectangle/sphere). Used for spatial indexing in games and simulations. Visualize the tree structure.

title: Convex Hull (Graham Scan)
desc: Compute the convex hull of a set of 2D points using Graham Scan. Find the lowest point (smallest y, then x), sort remaining by polar angle, scan to remove non-left-turn points. Output the hull vertices in order. Optionally visualize with points and hull polygon.

title: Hash Table from Scratch (open addressing)
desc: A hash table from scratch using open addressing (linear probing, quadratic probing, or double hashing). Implement put(key, value), get(key), remove(key), containsKey(key). Handle collisions with probing, resize table when load factor exceeds threshold (e.g. 0.75), rehash all entries.

title: Fast Fourier Transform (Cooley-Tukey)
desc: Implement the Fast Fourier Transform (Cooley-Tukey algorithm, radix-2 decimation-in-time). Recursively split sequence into even/odd indices, combine with twiddle factors. Compute magnitude spectrum from result. Support inverse FFT. Test with known signals (sine waves, square waves).

title: Simplex Algorithm (linear programming)
desc: The Simplex algorithm for linear programming. Given a system of linear constraints and an objective function (canonical form), find the optimal solution by moving between vertices of the feasible polytope. Handle maximization/minimization, slack variables, pivot selection (Bland's rule), and detect unbounded/infeasible cases.

title: Persistent Data Structures (immutable, versioned)
desc: Persistent (immutable) data structures that preserve previous versions when modified. Implement persistent lists (linked list with path copying), maps (hash array mapped trie - HAMT), and trees. Each modification creates a new structure sharing most of the old structure. Support version traversal.

title: WMS Map Viewer (non-web-based)
desc: A WMS (Web Map Service) viewer (non-web, desktop app). Send GetMap requests to a WMS server (e.g. USGS, OpenStreetMap WMS), parse XML capabilities, render returned map tiles, support zoom/pan, coordinate display (lat/lng), layer selection, and tile caching.

title: Lambert's Problem Solver (orbital mechanics)
desc: Solve Lambert's problem in orbital mechanics: given two position vectors and a transfer time, find the orbit connecting them. Implement the universal variable formulation with Newton's method to solve for transfer angle. Output orbital elements of the transfer trajectory. Handle both prograde and retrograde cases.

title: SAT Solver (DPLL algorithm)
desc: A SAT solver implementing the DPLL (Davis-Putnam-Logemann-Loveland) algorithm. Parse CNF (Conjunctive Normal Form) from DIMACS format. Apply unit propagation and pure literal elimination. Use recursive backtracking with conflict-driven clause learning (CDCL) for efficiency. Output SAT/UNSAT with satisfying assignment.

title: Markov Chain Sentence Generator
desc: A Markov chain text generator. Build a transition matrix from training text: for each k-word prefix (order k), record all possible next words with probabilities. Generate text by starting from a seed prefix and randomly picking the next word based on probabilities. Support variable randomness (temperature scaling).

title: To-Do List Application (web app or CLI)
desc: A to-do list app (web or CLI). CRUD operations for tasks with fields: title, description, due date, priority, category/ tags, status (pending/completed). Mark tasks complete, filter by status/priority/category, sort by due date/priority. Persistent storage (JSON file or database). CLI: add, list, done, delete.

title: Chatbot (conversation retention)
desc: A chatbot that retains conversation context. Accept user input, respond with rule-based pattern matching (ELIZA-like) or a simple ML model (seq2seq, retrieval-based). Maintain conversation history within a session. Support multiple topics and basic memory (user name, preferences).

title: Paint Clone (drawing tools, save/load images)
desc: A Paint clone with drawing tools: pencil, brush, eraser, fill (flood fill), shapes (line, rectangle, circle, polygon), color picker, adjustable brush size. Save/load images (BMP/PNG). Canvas with zoom. Use canvas API or framebuffer with pixel manipulation.

title: Graphic Calculator (plot functions, derivatives bonus)
desc: A graphic calculator that plots mathematical functions y = f(x). Input expression string, parse and evaluate. Draw coordinate axes with tick marks, grid lines. Plot functions as connected line segments. Support zoom and pan. Optionally compute and plot derivative and integral (numerical).

title: MIDI Player + Editor (play, edit, compose)
