desc: A MIDI player and editor. Parse standard MIDI files (SMF format: header, track chunks, variable-length delta time, MIDI events like Note On/Off, Program Change, Control Change). Play sound using synthesizer or soundfonts. Editor: add/remove/edit notes in a piano-roll interface.

title: English Sentence Parser (context analysis)
desc: An English sentence parser that performs context analysis. Tokenize into words, tag parts of speech (POS tagging using HMM or averaged perceptron), parse sentence structure (constituency or dependency parsing). Display parse tree and grammatical relationships between words.

title: Parametric/Graphic Equalizer (.wav files)
desc: A parametric/graphic equalizer for .wav files. Read WAV file (PCM samples), apply bandpass filters for each frequency band (bass, mid, treble) using IIR or FIR filters, adjust gain per band based on user settings, mix bands back together, write output WAV. Visualize frequency response curve.

title: Verlet Integration (cloth simulation)
desc: Cloth simulation using Verlet integration. Model cloth as a grid of particles connected by distance constraints (springs). Each particle has position, previous position. Apply gravity, wind, and collision (sphere, plane). Constrain distances with iterative relaxation. Render as a mesh with shading.

title: Sound Synthesis (sine, square, sawtooth, etc.)
desc: Sound synthesis that generates audio waveforms: sine, square (Fourier series), sawtooth, triangle, and noise (white, pink). Control frequency, amplitude, duration. Add envelope (ADSR: attack, decay, sustain, release). Output as .wav file or play via audio API. Support frequency modulation (FM synthesis).

title: Music Streaming Server
desc: A music streaming server that serves audio files over HTTP. Index a music library by scanning directories for audio files (MP3, FLAC, OGG). Serve a web UI for browsing by artist/album/track, play queue, and streaming via progressive download or HTTP range requests. Support playlists.

title: Shazam-like Audio Fingerprinting
desc: An audio fingerprinting system similar to Shazam. Record/read audio, compute spectrogram (FFT over sliding windows), find local peaks in time-frequency space, generate hashes from peak pairs. Match hashes against a database of known songs using combinatorial scoring. Return the best match with time offset.

title: TI-86 Graphing Calculator Emulator
desc: A TI-86 graphing calculator emulator. Implement the Z80 CPU (registers, instructions, interrupts), LCD display (128x64 pixel framebuffer), keypad input mapped to computer keyboard, and ROM loading. Emulate enough of the calculator to run built-in functions and graphing.

title: HTTP/3 (QUIC) Client
desc: An HTTP/3 client that uses QUIC (Quick UDP Internet Connections) instead of TCP. Implement QUIC connection establishment (handshake with transport parameters, TLS 1.3 integration), multiplexed streams (similar to HTTP/2), stream flow control, and 0-RTT resumption. Handle connection migration across IP addresses.

title: Server-Sent Events (SSE) Push Server
desc: A server that pushes events to clients using Server-Sent Events (SSE, EventSource API). Clients subscribe via HTTP and receive a stream of events (text/event-stream format). Support event types, reconnection with Last-Event-ID, and broadcasting events to all subscribed clients.

title: WebRTC Data Channel File Sharing
desc: A file sharing application using WebRTC Data Channels for peer-to-peer transfer. Establish WebRTC connection (STUN/TURN for NAT traversal, SDP offer/answer exchange via signaling server). Implement reliable and unordered data channels, chunk large files, and show transfer progress.

title: REST API Client Library
desc: A REST API client library that wraps HTTP requests with a fluent interface. Support all HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH), headers, query parameters, request body (JSON/form-data), authentication (Basic, Bearer, API key), automatic JSON parsing, error handling, and retries.

title: OpenAPI / Swagger Spec Parser
desc: An OpenAPI/Swagger spec parser that reads OpenAPI 3.0/3.1 YAML/JSON specifications. Parse paths, operations, parameters, request bodies, responses, schemas (with $ref resolution), security schemes, and servers. Validate a request/response against the spec. Generate client code or documentation.

title: Webhook Receiver & Dispatcher Service
desc: A webhook receiver and dispatcher service. Accept incoming HTTP POST requests at a registered URL, validate via HMAC signature, queue them, and dispatch to configured subscriber endpoints with retries (exponential backoff). Provide a dashboard for delivery logs and manual retry.

title: Rate Limiter (Token Bucket / Leaky Bucket)
desc: A rate limiter implementing token bucket or leaky bucket algorithm. Token bucket: tokens added at a fixed rate, each request consumes one. Leaky bucket: requests added to a queue, processed at fixed rate. Support per-user and per-IP limits. Expose current rate limit status headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset).

title: Circuit Breaker Pattern Library
desc: A circuit breaker pattern library. Track recent failures. When failure count exceeds threshold, trip to open state (requests fail fast). After a timeout, transition to half-open (allow limited requests). On success, close circuit; on failure, reopen. Support configurable thresholds, timeouts, and custom fallback handlers.

title: Service Discovery Registry (Consul-like)
desc: A service discovery registry similar to Consul. Services register with a name, address, port, and health check endpoint. Clients query by service name to get healthy instances. Implement health checking (periodic TCP/HTTP checks), TTL-based expiration of dead services, and DNS SRV record interface.

title: Distributed Tracing System (Jaeger-like)
desc: A distributed tracing system like Jaeger. Instrument applications to emit spans (operation name, start/end timestamps, parent span ID, tags). Collect spans via a agent/collector, store them, and provide a query API. Build a UI to visualize traces as flame graphs / waterfall charts showing service call hierarchy and latency.

title: Web Push Notification Service
desc: A web push notification service implementing the Web Push API (RFC 8292, VAPID). Accept subscription objects from browsers, encrypt payloads with VAPID keys, send to push service endpoints (FCM, Mozilla autopush). Handle subscription expiration and update. Provide a REST API to trigger notifications.

title: Long-Polling Chat Server
desc: A chat server using long-polling: clients send regular HTTP requests to check for new messages, server holds the response until a message arrives or timeout occurs. Implement message queues per user, FIFO delivery, and simple room/channel support. Include a basic HTML/JS client.

title: CQRS / Event Sourcing Framework
desc: A CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) / Event Sourcing framework. Separate write commands from read queries. Store state changes as an event stream. Rebuild read models by replaying events. Support snapshots for performance. Implement event buses for pub/sub communication between aggregates.

title: Health Check & Status Dashboard
desc: A health check and status dashboard. Periodically ping configured endpoints/services (HTTP, TCP, ping), check response time and status code. Display a dashboard with green/red indicators, response time charts, uptime percentage, and alert on consecutive failures. Support notification via email/webhook.

title: HTTP Proxy with Caching (Squid-like)
desc: An HTTP proxy server with caching similar to Squid. Forward HTTP requests from clients to destination servers, cache responses based on Cache-Control headers (max-age, must-revalidate). Implement cache eviction (LRU), stale content serving with validation (If-Modified-Since, ETag), and access control lists.

title: IPFS-like Distributed File System Node
desc: An IPFS-like distributed file system node. Implement a content-addressed block storage (MerkleDAG), Kademlia DHT for peer discovery and content routing, BitSwap protocol for block exchange between peers, and IPNS for mutable pointers. Support file add/get commands via CLI.

title: eBPF Program (packet filter / tracing)
desc: An eBPF program that attaches to kernel hooks (XDP, tracepoints, kprobes). Write in C, compile to BPF bytecode. Implement a packet filter that drops/monitors packets by IP/port, or a tracing program that counts syscalls per process. Use bpftool or libbpf to load and attach the program.

title: Unikernel (single-address-space OS image)
desc: A unikernel: a single-address-space machine image that runs directly on hardware or hypervisor without OS. Compile the application with minimal runtime (no process separation, no syscalls). Boot via multiboot protocol, implement minimal device drivers (console, network), and run on QEMU/KVM.

title: Simple Type-2 Hypervisor
desc: A simple Type-2 hypervisor that runs on a host OS using KVM. Set up VM using KVM API (/dev/kvm), allocate VM memory, configure vCPU with CPUID/MSR, load a kernel (Linux bzImage or custom bare-metal binary), handle VM exits (I/O, MMIO, HLT), and provide a console for VM interaction.

title: Network Filesystem (NFS-like)
desc: A network filesystem (NFS-like) that allows a client to mount a remote directory. Server exports a directory, handles RPC calls (LOOKUP, READ, WRITE, CREATE, REMOVE, RENAME, READDIR) over a custom protocol on TCP. Client implements a FUSE filesystem that translates filesystem calls to RPC requests.

title: RAM Disk Driver
desc: A RAM disk driver that creates a block device backed by system memory. Allocate a buffer in kernel memory, implement read/write operations (copy from/to user space), handle block I/O requests, and register as a standard block device. Format with a filesystem and mount to access.

title: /proc Filesystem Implementation
desc: A /proc-like filesystem that exposes kernel data structures as virtual files. Implement read operations: /uptime (system uptime), /meminfo (memory usage), /cpuinfo (CPU info), /version (kernel version). Use a FUSE filesystem or kernel module to create the virtual files that return real-time data on read.

title: Kernel Module for GPIO Control
desc: A Linux kernel module that controls GPIO pins via sysfs or gpiod interface. Implement export/unexport of GPIOs, set direction (input/output), read/write values, and handle interrupts (poll for edge detection). Accept commands via a character device or sysfs attributes.

title: KVM-based Lightweight Emulator
desc: A lightweight emulator using KVM for hardware acceleration. Open /dev/kvm, create VM and vCPU, set up guest memory with identity mapping, load a minimal kernel/binary, run vCPU with KVM_RUN, handle exits (port I/O, MMIO) with emulated devices (UART console, timer).

title: NUMA-Aware Memory Allocator
desc: A NUMA-aware memory allocator. Detect NUMA topology via sysfs or libnuma. Allocate memory from the local NUMA node for threads (first-touch policy). Maintain per-node free lists. Implement migration of pages between nodes based on access patterns. Use mbind/move_pages syscalls.

title: ACPI Driver (power management basics)
desc: An ACPI driver that handles power management basics. Parse ACPI tables (RSDP, RSDT, DSDT, FADT), implement the AML interpreter for control methods, support S-state transitions (sleep/wake), and handle power button events via ACPI fixed events.

title: Interrupt Controller (PIC/APIC simulation)
desc: An interrupt controller simulation (PIC 8259A or APIC). For PIC: handle IRQ lines, priority resolution, masking, cascading, and interrupt acknowledge cycle. For APIC: implement local APIC (timer, LINT0/LINT1, IPI) and I/O APIC (redirection entries, interrupt routing).

title: PCI Device Driver
desc: A PCI device driver that enumerates PCI devices on the bus by scanning bus/device/function numbers, reads PCI configuration space (vendor/device ID, class code, BARs), enables bus mastering and MMIO, maps BAR regions, and registers an interrupt handler. Write to a QEMU emulated device.

title: Scheduler Visualization Tool
desc: A visualization tool for OS process scheduling. Simulate a schedule (FCFS, SJF, Round-Robin, Priority, MLFQ) given a list of processes with arrival time and burst time. Show Gantt chart, compute average turnaround time, waiting time, and response time. Optionally animate the schedule execution.

title: Core Dump Analyzer
desc: A core dump analyzer that reads ELF core files and extracts information. Parse program headers, read memory segments, locate thread stacks, resolve function names (if debug symbols available), show register state at crash, and display stack backtrace from the saved stack frames.

title: Performance Counter Monitor (perf-like)
desc: A performance counter monitor similar to perf. Use perf_event_open syscall to read hardware/software PMU counters: CPU cycles, instructions, cache misses, branches, branch misses, context switches, page faults. Profile a target program and output per-function or per-instruction counts.

title: User-Space Network Stack (netmap-like)
desc: A user-space network stack using netmap or DPDK. Bypass the kernel network stack for high packet processing. Implement packet I/O in a loop: read packets from NIC rings, parse Ethernet/IP/UDP/TCP headers, handle ARP/ICMP, and optionally implement a simple TCP stack with sliding window.

title: Triplestore (RDF/SPARQL Database)
desc: An RDF triplestore with SPARQL query support. Store subject-predicate-object triples in an indexed format (six-way indexing). Parse SPARQL queries (SELECT, CONSTRUCT, ASK, DESCRIBE), implement basic graph patterns, FILTERs, OPTIONALs, and ORDER BY. Support Turtle/N-Triples import/export.

title: Document Store (MongoDB-like)
desc: A document store similar to MongoDB. Store JSON-like documents in collections with a dynamic schema. Support CRUD operations, indexing (single-field, compound, text), aggregation pipeline ($match, $group, $sort, $project), and a simple query language with operators ($gt, $lt, $in, $regex).

title: Wide-Column Store (Cassandra-like)
desc: A wide-column store like Apache Cassandra. Distribute data across nodes using consistent hashing (partition key), replicate for fault tolerance, support column families with flexible columns, implement CQL (Cassandra Query Language) subset, and handle node addition/removal with hinted handoff and repair.

title: In-Memory Data Grid (Hazelcast-like)
desc: An in-memory data grid similar to Hazelcast. Distribute objects across cluster nodes with partitioning and replication. Support distributed data structures (map, list, queue, set with atomic operations), entry listeners for change notifications, and distributed locking with TTL.

title: Streaming Database (Materialize-like)
desc: A streaming database similar to Materialize that maintains materialized views on streaming data. Ingest data from Kafka topics or append-only logs, incrementally update views as new data arrives, support SQL queries on streaming data with window functions, and emit updated results.

title: Change Data Capture (CDC) System
desc: A Change Data Capture (CDC) system that tails a database's WAL (Write-Ahead Log) or binlog and streams row-level changes. Parse WAL records (insert, update, delete with before/after images), serialize changes to Avro/JSON, and publish to Kafka or similar. Support PostgreSQL WAL decoding or MySQL binlog.

title: Database Migration Tool
desc: A database migration tool that manages schema versioning. Maintain a versions table in the database. Support up/down migrations written in SQL or code. Track which migrations have been applied, apply pending migrations in order, and rollback on error. CLI or library with support for multiple database engines.

title: ORM (Object-Relational Mapper) from Scratch
desc: An ORM (Object-Relational Mapper) from scratch. Map classes/tables with annotations/metadata: columns, primary keys, foreign keys, relationships (one-to-many, many-to-many), indexes. Generate SQL CRUD statements automatically from method calls. Support lazy loading, eager loading (JOINs), and migrations.

title: Query Optimizer / Query Planner
desc: A query optimizer / query planner for a relational database. Parse SQL, generate a logical plan (algebraic operators: scan, filter, join, project, aggregate), apply optimization rules (predicate pushdown, projection pushdown, join reordering, constant folding), estimate costs using statistics, and generate a physical plan.

title: Multi-Version Concurrency Control (MVCC)
desc: Multi-Version Concurrency Control (MVCC) implementation. Each transaction sees a snapshot of the database at its start time. Store multiple versions of each row (with creation/deletion timestamps or transaction IDs). Handle write-write conflicts with first-committer-wins or SI. Implement garbage collection of old versions.

title: Write-Ahead Log (WAL) Implementation
desc: A Write-Ahead Log (WAL) for crash recovery. Before modifying data pages, append a log record (transaction ID, operation, before/after image) to the log. On crash, replay the log to restore consistency (redo committed transactions, undo uncommitted ones). Support log truncation after checkpoint.

title: Distributed Transaction Coordinator (2PC)
desc: A distributed transaction coordinator implementing Two-Phase Commit (2PC). Coordinator sends prepare requests to all participants, waits for votes. If all vote yes, send commit; otherwise send abort. Handle coordinator failure with recovery protocol. Optionally implement Three-Phase Commit (3PC) for non-blocking behavior.

title: Sharded Counter Service
desc: A sharded counter service for high-throughput counting. Distribute counter increments across multiple shards (keys hashed to shards). Use quorum writes on replicas within each shard. Read merges all shard values. Handle concurrent increments without locks via CRDT counters per shard.

title: HyperLogLog (cardinality estimation)
desc: HyperLogLog for cardinality estimation of large multisets using limited memory. Apply hash function to each element, estimate cardinality from the maximum number of leading zeros in the hash. Implement harmonic mean of multiple registers for accuracy. Support merge of independent HLL structures.

title: Geospatial Index (R-Tree / Quad-Tree)
desc: A geospatial index using R-Tree or Quad-Tree for spatial queries. Insert 2D bounding boxes, support range queries (find all objects within a rectangle) and nearest-neighbor queries. R-Tree: split nodes on overflow, balance tree. Quad-Tree: recursively subdivide space into quadrants.

title: Count-Min Sketch (frequency estimation)
desc: A Count-Min Sketch for frequency estimation in data streams. Use a 2D array of counters with d hash functions. For each element, increment counters at positions h_i(element). Estimate frequency as minimum of all counter values. Support point queries and heavy hitter detection.

title: Genetic Algorithm Framework
desc: A genetic algorithm framework. Define Individuals (chromosomes with genes), fitness function, selection (tournament, roulette wheel), crossover (single/multi-point, uniform), mutation (bit flip, swap, Gaussian). Run evolution with configurable population size, generations, and elitism.

title: Particle Swarm Optimization
desc: Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) for global optimization. Each particle has position and velocity in search space. Update velocity as weighted sum of inertia, personal best attraction, and global best attraction. Evaluate fitness, update personal/global bests. Benchmark on standard test functions (Rastrigin, Sphere, Rosenbrock).

title: Bayesian Network / Belief Network
desc: A Bayesian network / belief network: a DAG where nodes represent random variables and edges represent conditional dependencies. Implement variable elimination for exact inference, incorporate conditional probability tables, support evidence insertion, and compute marginal/conditional probabilities.

title: Hidden Markov Model (HMM) from Scratch
desc: A Hidden Markov Model (HMM) from scratch. Implement the Forward algorithm (probability of observation sequence), Viterbi algorithm (most likely state sequence), and Baum-Welch algorithm (EM for parameter estimation: initial probabilities, transition matrix, emission matrix). Test on POS tagging or DNA sequence analysis.

title: Word2Vec / GloVe Embeddings from Scratch
desc: Train Word2Vec (word embeddings) from scratch using skip-gram or CBOW. Build a neural network with one hidden layer, train by predicting context words (skip-gram) or target word from context (CBOW) using negative sampling. Output word vectors. Also implement GloVe: build co-occurrence matrix, factorize via weighted least squares.

title: Named Entity Recognition (NER) System
desc: A Named Entity Recognition (NER) system that identifies entities (person, organization, location, date, etc.) in text. Use a BiLSTM-CRF or transformer-based model. Train on CoNLL-2003 or similar dataset. Tag each token with BIO (Begin, Inside, Outside) entity labels. Evaluate with F1 score.

title: Extractive Text Summarizer
desc: An extractive text summarizer that selects the most important sentences from a document. Use TF-IDF-based sentence ranking (sentence score = sum of word TF-IDF scores) or TextRank (PageRank on sentence similarity graph). Select top-k sentences maintaining coherence. Evaluate using ROUGE scores.

title: Question Answering System (BERT-style)
desc: A question answering system (BERT-style) that reads a passage and answers questions. Fine-tune a pre-trained BERT model on SQuAD: add a linear layer predicting answer start and end positions from the passage-encoded representation. Support both extractive and abstractive answer generation.

title: Document Clustering (HDBSCAN)
desc: Document clustering using HDBSCAN (Hierarchical Density-Based Spatial Clustering). Convert documents to TF-IDF vectors or embeddings, compute pairwise distances, build a minimum spanning tree, extract clusters by varying density threshold. Handle noise points and visualize with t-SNE.

title: Isolation Forest (Anomaly Detection)
desc: Isolation Forest for anomaly detection. Build an ensemble of random trees: recursively partition data by randomly selecting a feature and split value. Anomalies require fewer partitions to isolate, so they have shorter path lengths. Score anomalies by average path length across trees.

title: Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM)
desc: A Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) for clustering. Model data as a weighted sum of K Gaussian components. Implement the Expectation-Maximization (EM) algorithm: E-step computes posterior component responsibilities, M-step updates means, covariances, and mixing weights. Detect convergence by log-likelihood change.

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) from Scratch
desc: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) from scratch. Center the data, compute covariance matrix, find eigenvalues/eigenvectors (via power iteration or SVD), sort by eigenvalue magnitude, project data onto top-k principal components. Use for dimensionality reduction and visualization.

title: t-SNE Dimensionality Reduction
desc: t-SNE for dimensionality reduction to 2D/3D for visualization. Compute pairwise high-dimensional similarities (Gaussian), define low-dimensional similarities (Student-t), minimize KL divergence between the two distributions using gradient descent with momentum. Use Barnes-Hut approximation for efficient computation.

title: Gradient Boosting Machine (XGBoost-like)
desc: A gradient boosting machine similar to XGBoost. Build an ensemble of decision trees sequentially: each tree fits the negative gradient (pseudo-residuals) of the loss function. Implement regularization (learning rate, subsampling, column sampling, tree depth limit, min child weight). Support regression, classification, and ranking objectives.

title: Latent Dirichlet Allocation (Topic Modeling)
desc: Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) for topic modeling. Represent each document as a distribution over topics and each topic as a distribution over words. Implement collapsed Gibbs sampling: iteratively re-sample topic assignments for each word based on current counts. Output top words per topic and topic proportions per document.

title: Causal Inference / A/B Testing Framework
desc: An A/B testing / causal inference framework. Apply statistical tests (t-test, chi-squared, Mann-Whitney U) to compare control and treatment groups. Compute effect size, confidence intervals, p-values. Handle multiple testing correction (Bonferroni, FDR). Implement stratified sampling and power analysis for sample size determination.

title: Forth Language Interpreter
desc: A Forth language interpreter. Forth uses a stack-based, concatenative, postfix paradigm with a dictionary of words. Implement the inner interpreter (execution token model), stack operations (DUP, DROP, SWAP, OVER, ROT), arithmetic, control structures (IF, ELSE, THEN, DO LOOP), defining words (:), and a REPL.

title: Prolog Inference Engine
desc: A Prolog inference engine implementing depth-first SLD resolution. Parse Horn clauses, perform unification (with occurs check), resolution via backtracking with a choice-point stack. Support facts, rules, queries, lists, arithmetic, and cut (!). Implement a simple REPL with trace facility showing proofs.

title: Smalltalk-like VM / Object System
desc: A Smalltalk-like virtual machine / object system. Everything is an object. Implement the bytecode interpreter, object memory (object headers, instance variables, method dictionary), message sending (dynamic dispatch), inheritance, and garbage collection. Include a small image with basic classes.

title: Brainfuck JIT Compiler
desc: A Brainfuck JIT compiler that generates x86_64 machine code at runtime. Translate Brainfuck commands (> < + - . , [ ]) to native x86_64 instructions. Use mmap with PROT_EXEC for generated code. Optimize loops by collapsing repeated commands and skipping null loops. Fill a buffer, execute, return results.

title: Python Bytecode Disassembler
desc: A Python bytecode disassembler that reads .pyc files (Marshall format) and decodes Python bytecode instructions. Parse the code object, decode each opcode (LOAD_CONST, BINARY_ADD, CALL_FUNCTION, etc.), show line numbers, instruction offset, opcode name, and arguments. Display the bytecode as a readable listing.

title: SQL Parser (full grammar)
desc: An SQL parser that parses a full SQL grammar. Tokenize SQL into keywords, identifiers, operators, literals, punctuation. Build an AST using recursive descent or yacc: SELECT (projections, FROM, JOIN, WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, ORDER BY, LIMIT), INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE TABLE. Support subqueries and aliases.

title: YAML Parser from Scratch
desc: A YAML parser from scratch. Handle YAML 1.2 core types: mappings (key: value), sequences (- item), strings (plain, quoted, block scalar with | or >), booleans (true/false), numbers (int, float, hex, octal), null, anchors (&) and aliases (*), multi-document (---). Support nested structures.

title: TOML Parser from Scratch
desc: A TOML parser from scratch (TOML v1.0). Parse key-value pairs, tables ([table]), arrays of tables ([[array]]), data types: strings (basic, multi-line, literal), integers, floats, booleans, offsets datetimes, local datetimes, local dates, local times, arrays, inline tables. Handle dotted keys.

title: INI / Config File Parser
desc: An INI / config file parser. Parse sections [section], key=value pairs, comments (#, ;). Support quoted values, multi-line values (continuation lines), variable substitution (%(var)s). Output as a dictionary of sections to key-value maps. Handle Windows INI quirks (no section header = global).

title: C Preprocessor (macros, includes, pragma)
desc: A C preprocessor that handles #include (file search and insertion), #define (object-like and function-like macros with arguments), #undef, #if/#ifdef/#ifndef/#else/#elif/#endif (conditional compilation with constant expression evaluation), #pragma (implementation-defined), and #error/#warning.

title: Source Map Generator (JS/CSS)
desc: A source map generator for JavaScript/CSS. Given the original source and the transformed/minified output, generate mappings encoded in the Source Map v3 format (VLQ-encoded base64). Track line/column mappings through transformations. Support names and sources arrays.

title: Simple Decompiler (binary to pseudocode)
desc: A simple decompiler that reads binary executables and produces pseudocode. Parse ELF/PE headers, disassemble machine code (using Capstone or similar), lift to an intermediate representation (IR), build a control-flow graph (CFG), recover structured control flow (if/else/while), and emit C-like pseudocode.

title: Debug Symbol Reader (DWARF/PDB)
desc: A DWARF/PDB debug symbol reader. Parse DWARF sections (.debug_info, .debug_abbrev, .debug_line, .debug_str) to extract compilation units, types, functions, variables, line number tables. For PDB: parse MSF (Multi-Stream Format), DBI stream (modules, sections), and TPI stream (types). Map addresses to source locations.

title: S-Expression Parser + Pretty Printer
desc: An S-Expression parser and pretty printer. Parse Lisp-like S-expressions: atoms (symbols, numbers, strings) and lists (parenthesized, dot notation (a . b)). Pretty-printer formats S-expressions with proper indentation, line breaking, and optional color output. Support comments (;) and circular structures (#n= / #n#).

title: PEG Parser Generator
desc: A PEG (Parsing Expression Grammar) parser generator. Define grammar rules (ordered choice /, sequence, zero-or-more *, one-or-more +, optional ?, and-predicate &, not-predicate !). Generate a recursive descent parser from the grammar. Support left-recursion elimination and packrat parsing with memoization for linear time.

title: Language Server Protocol (LSP) Implementation
desc: A Language Server Protocol (LSP) implementation for a programming language. Implement the stdio-based JSON-RPC transport, handle requests (initialize, textDocument/completion, hover, gotoDefinition, references, documentHighlight, formatting). Maintain a document workspace, parse documents, and provide analysis results.

title: GLTF/GLB 3D Model Loader
desc: A GLTF/GLB 3D model loader that loads the glTF 2.0 format (JSON descriptor + binary buffers). Parse nodes, meshes, materials (PBR metallic-roughness), textures, animations (keyframe-based with interpolation), skins (skeletal animation with joint matrices). Upload data to GPU for rendering.

title: Bezier Curve Editor
desc: A Bézier curve editor. Allow the user to place and drag control points. Draw the cubic Bézier curve (or quadratic, linear) using the De Casteljau algorithm. Support multiple curves connected with C1/G1 continuity. Export curves as SVG paths.

title: Subdivision Surface Modeler (Catmull-Clark)
desc: A subdivision surface modeler implementing Catmull-Clark subdivision for arbitrary topology meshes. Repeatedly subdivide faces: each face->n quads, each vertex->new vertex, compute face/average points. Implement crease/sharp edge handling, and limit surface evaluation. Export models as OBJ.

title: Marching Cubes (isosurface extraction)
desc: Marching Cubes for extracting an isosurface from a 3D scalar field. Sample field values at grid points, for each cube cell determine which edges the surface crosses (lookup in precomputed edge table), generate triangle mesh. Apply linear interpolation along edges for smooth surface. Use on implicit functions or volume data.

title: Terrain Generation (Perlin Noise + Erosion)
desc: Procedural terrain generation using Perlin/Simplex noise for height maps, then apply erosion simulation (thermal and hydraulic). Hydraulic erosion: simulate water flow, sediment transport (dissolve, carry, deposit). Render terrain with colored height-map shading, optionally as 3D mesh.

title: Fluid Simulation (SPH / PIC / FLIP)
desc: A fluid simulation using SPH (Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics), PIC (Particle-In-Cell), or FLIP (Fluid-Implicit-Particle). Simulate particles with pressure, viscosity, and surface tension forces. Use spatial hashing for neighbor search. Render as particles or surface mesh. Handle solid boundaries.

title: Cloth Simulation (Spring-Mass System)
desc: A cloth simulation using a spring-mass system. Model cloth as a grid of masses connected by structural, shear, and bend springs with damping. Integrate using Verlet or Runge-Kutta. Apply gravity, wind force, and pin constraints. Handle collision with spheres and planes. Use spatial grid for self-collision.

title: Volumetric Cloud / Fog Rendering
desc: Volumetric cloud / fog rendering using ray marching. Define a 3D density field (Perlin/Worley noise). For each screen pixel, march a ray through the volume, accumulate color and opacity (transmittance). Apply exponential height fog and Henyey-Greenstein phase function for scattering. Render to screen.

title: Global Illumination (Path Tracing)
desc: Global illumination using path tracing. For each pixel, trace rays from the camera, bounce through the scene accumulating radiance via Monte Carlo integration. Implement diffuse and specular BRDFs, importance sampling, Russian roulette for termination, and next-event estimation (direct light sampling).

title: Shadow Mapping (Cascaded / PCF)
desc: Shadow mapping: render the scene from the light's perspective into a depth buffer (shadow map). In the main render pass, compare each fragment's depth against the shadow map to determine if it's in shadow. Implement cascaded shadow maps (CSM) for large scenes and PCF (percentage-closer filtering) for soft shadows.

title: Screen-Space Reflections
desc: Screen-space reflections (SSR). For each fragment, compute the reflection ray direction from the view normal. Ray-march in screen space (sampling the depth buffer) to find the intersection point. Reflect the scene color at the intersection, blending based on roughness and distance.

title: Post-Processing Pipeline (Bloom, DOF, Motion Blur)
desc: A post-processing pipeline with bloom, depth of field (DOF), and motion blur. Bloom: extract bright pixels, blur (Gaussian), add back to original. DOF: blur based on distance from focal plane (bokeh). Motion blur: sample along velocity vector per pixel (from previous frame's transformation).

title: GPU Compute Shader (particles / physics)
desc: A GPU compute shader for particle physics or simulation. Use Vulkan/Metal/DirectX compute shaders or OpenGL compute shaders. Implement a particle system entirely on GPU: update positions/velocities in a compute shader, render particles from the same buffer. Use atomics for interactions.

title: Signed Distance Field (SDF) Renderer
desc: A Signed Distance Field (SDF) renderer using ray marching. Define 3D primitives as SDFs (sphere, box, torus, cylinder, cone) and combine them with union/subtraction/intersection operations. March rays through the scene, compute normals via SDF gradient, apply Phong lighting and ambient occlusion.

title: Color Quantization / Palette Extraction
desc: Color quantization / palette extraction. Reduce the number of colors in an image using k-means clustering on RGB pixels, median cut (recursively split color box at median), or octree quantization. Output the reduced-color image and the extracted palette. Compare results for different numbers of colors.

title: Procedural City / Building Generator
desc: A procedural city/building generator. Generate road networks (grid-based or radial), subdivide into blocks, place buildings with random heights/styling (L-systems or extrusion from footprints), add props (trees, lamps). Output as a 3D scene (OBJ) or city layout data.

title: Visual Novel Engine (Ren'Py-like)
desc: A visual novel engine similar to Ren'Py. Display character sprites and backgrounds with layered positions. Show dialogue text with typewriter effect. Support branching choices that affect story path, save/load game state, and transitions (fade, dissolve). Read story scripts from a plain text markup format.

title: Point-and-Click Adventure Engine
desc: A point-and-click adventure engine. Navigate scenes with click-to-move, interact with objects (examine, use, combine, talk), manage inventory, trigger dialog trees, and solve puzzles. Render 2D scenes with walkable areas (walk boxes) and object hotspots.

title: Interactive Fiction / Text Parser (Zork-like)
desc: Interactive fiction / text parser engine like Zork. Parse natural language commands (GO NORTH, TAKE KEY, OPEN DOOR, INVENTORY). Implement a world model with rooms, objects, and NPCs. Define actions and their preconditions. Handle basic text parsing (verb-noun, verb-preposition-noun).

title: Sokoban / Puzzle Game Engine
desc: A Sokoban / puzzle game engine. Player pushes boxes onto target points in a grid-based warehouse. Implement movement, box pushing (only one at a time, not through walls), undo (with move history), level loading from standard Sokoban format (RLE, #$.*@+), and auto-solve using BFS.

title: Rhythm Game (Guitar Hero / osu!-like)
desc: A rhythm game similar to Guitar Hero or osu!. Notes scroll down/toward the player; player must press the correct key/tap the note in time. Implement beat map loading (timing, position, type), scoring (accuracy-based combo system), visual feedback (miss/hit/good/perfect), and song playback synchronized to notes.

title: Top-Down Racing Game
desc: A top-down racing game. Track as a 2D circuit with walls, checkpoints, start/finish line. Car physics: acceleration, braking, steering with momentum/slip, collision with walls. AI opponents follow a racing line. Laps, positions, and timers. Mini-map showing positions on the track.

title: Fighting Game Engine (Street Fighter-like)
desc: A fighting game engine (Street Fighter-like). Characters with animations (idle, walk, punch, kick, special move), hitbox/hurtbox collision detection, health bar, combo system (cancel chains), input buffering for special moves (quarter-circle forward + punch), round system with timer.

title: Tower Defense Game
desc: A tower defense game. Enemies follow a path from start to goal. Player places towers along the path that auto-attack enemies in range. Implement multiple tower types (arrow, cannon, magic, slow), upgradeable damage/range/speed, enemy wave spawning with increasing difficulty, and lives system.

title: City Builder / Tycoon Game
desc: A city builder / tycoon game. 2D top-down grid-based. Zones (residential, commercial, industrial) that develop over time based on demand, services (power, water, roads, police, fire, parks), budget management (tax rate vs spending), population growth, and happiness metrics.

title: Idle / Incremental Clicker Game
desc: An idle/incremental clicker game. Player clicks to earn currency, buys auto-generators that produce currency over time (e.g., cursor, grandma, farm, mine in Cookie Clicker style). Implement prestige system (reset for permanent bonuses), upgrade multiplicative bonuses, progression feedback, and save/load.

title: Battle Royale (simplified, AI opponents)
desc: A simplified battle royale game with AI opponents. 2D top-down, player and AI land on a map, loot weapons/items (procedural loot distribution), shrinking safe zone (circle system), combat with projectiles, last alive wins. AI uses explore-collect-fight behavior.

title: Online Multiplayer Lobby + Matchmaking
desc: An online multiplayer lobby and matchmaking system. Players connect to a lobby server, create/join rooms, signal ready. Matchmaking algorithm forms balanced teams based on ELO/skill rating. Game host starts match, server distributes game configuration to peers.

title: Collectible Card Game (Hearthstone-like)
desc: A collectible card game similar to Hearthstone. Cards with mana cost, attack, health, and abilities. Players have decks, draw cards, summon minions, cast spells. Implement turn phases (draw, play cards, attack, end turn), targeting, status effects, and win condition (reduce opponent HP to 0).

title: Hidden Object Game
desc: A hidden object game. A cluttered scene image is displayed, player must find listed objects by clicking on them. Click areas defined by region coordinates. Timer, hint system (highlight region briefly), score based on speed. Multiple levels with different scenes and object lists.

title: Endless Runner (Temple Run-like)
desc: An endless runner (Temple Run-like). 3D third-person perspective, player character runs forward automatically on a procedurally generated path. Swipe to turn left/right, jump, slide under obstacles. Coins collectible for score multiplier. Increasing speed over time. Game over on obstacle collision.

title: Pinball Simulation
desc: A pinball simulation. 2D ball physics: gravity, friction, bouncing off walls/paddles/bumpers (impulse-based collision response). Flipper mechanism (keyboard-controlled, rotates around a hinge). Bumpers, slingshots, ramps, targets, and scoring. Drain at bottom for lost ball. Multi-ball mode.

title: Differential Privacy Library
desc: A differential privacy library implementing mechanisms: Laplace (add Laplace noise to numeric query results), Gaussian (for Gaussian noise), Exponential (for discrete choices). Support epsilon-delta privacy, composition theorems (sequential, parallel, Rényi), and privacy budget tracking.

title: Homomorphic Encryption (simple addition)
desc: A homomorphic encryption library supporting simple addition on encrypted data (Paillier or BGV-style). Implement key generation, encryption, decryption, and homomorphic addition of ciphertexts. Demonstrate: encrypt two numbers, add them homomorphically, decrypt to show correct sum.

title: Shamir's Secret Sharing Scheme
desc: Shamir's Secret Sharing scheme. Split a secret into n shares such that any k shares can reconstruct the secret but k-1 reveal nothing. Use polynomial interpolation over a finite field (prime modulus): random polynomial of degree k-1 with secret as constant term, shares are points on the polynomial.

title: ECDSA Digital Signatures from Scratch
desc: ECDSA (Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm) from scratch. Implement elliptic curve arithmetic over prime fields (point addition, doubling, scalar multiplication using double-and-add). Use secp256k1 curve parameters. Implement key generation, signing (k generation, R and S computation), and verification.

title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Prototype (lattice)
desc: A post-quantum cryptography prototype using lattice-based cryptography (e.g., NewHope or CRYSTALS-Kyber). Key generation: generate matrix A and small error vectors s, e. Encryption: encapsulate shared secret using A and public key. Decryption: recover shared secret using private key. Demonstrate resistance to quantum attacks.

title: Side-Channel Attack Demo (timing/power)
desc: A side-channel attack demonstration. Timing attack: measure time to compare a password byte-by-byte, infer each byte by timing variations (earlier mismatch returns faster). Power analysis: measure power consumption during AES encryption to infer key bits (simple/differential power analysis).

title: Network Protocol Fuzzer (AFL-like)
desc: A network protocol fuzzer similar to AFL. Accept a target binary and an initial seed input. Mutate inputs (bit flips, arithmetic changes, block splicing, dictionary insertion) and feed to the target. Detect crashes and hangs. Track code coverage using compile-time instrumentation or QEMU to guide mutations.

title: Seccomp-Based Application Sandbox
desc: A seccomp-based application sandbox that restricts syscalls. Load a seccomp BPF filter to allow only a whitelist of syscalls (read, write, exit, mmap, etc.). Use seccomp() with SECCOMP_SET_MODE_FILTER. Kill or log on disallowed syscall. Optionally include a default-deny policy with per-application configuration.

title: Hardened Container Runtime (gVisor-like)
desc: A hardened container runtime similar to gVisor. Implement a user-space kernel that intercepts application syscalls (via ptrace or kvm) and handles them in a restricted Go/bare-metal runtime. Provide a Linux ABI without direct host kernel access. Implement file system (9P or overlay), networking (gvisor TCP stack), and process management.

title: Credential Vault / Secrets Hub
desc: A credential vault / secrets hub that stores secrets (API keys, passwords, certificates) encrypted at rest. Provide a REST API for CRUD operations, access control via tokens, audit logging of all access, secret rotation, versioning, and expiration. Integrate with Kubernetes (Secrets Store CSI driver) or CI/CD pipelines.

title: Secrets Rotation Service
desc: A secrets rotation service that periodically updates credentials across services. Define rotation policies (every 30 days). Support common credential types: database passwords, API keys, SSH keys, TLS certificates. Automatically generate new secrets, update target services, and verify the new credential works before deactivating the old one.

title: Code Signing & Verification System
desc: A code signing and verification system. Sign executables/binaries with a private key (RSA or ECDSA), embed the signature in the file (or as a detached signature). Verifier extracts the signature, computes the file hash, and checks against the public key. Support both PE (Authenticode) and ELF signing.

title: GPG-like Encryption Tool
desc: A GPG-like encryption tool using hybrid encryption. Generate PGP key pairs (RSA or ECC), encrypt files using a session key (AES-256) wrapped with the recipient's public key, sign with sender's private key. Support key generation, export/import, encryption/decryption, signing/verification, and key server operations.

title: Password Strength Estimator (zxcvbn-like)
desc: A password strength estimator similar to zxcvbn. Use pattern matching: dictionary words (common passwords, leaked passwords), keyboard patterns (qwerty, asdf), repeated characters, dates, sequences. Calculate entropy for each pattern. Estimate crack time (offline fast/slow hashing, online throttled). Output score 0-4 and suggestions.

title: Certificate Transparency Log Monitor
desc: A Certificate Transparency log monitor that watches CT logs for certificates issued on your domains. Fetch Signed Certificate Timestamps (SCTs) from CT log servers, parse certificates, check for unauthorized issuances, alert on suspicious certificates. Support log inclusion proof verification using Merkle trees.

title: Web Application Firewall (WAF) Rules Engine
desc: A Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules engine. Inspect HTTP requests (URL, headers, body) and apply rules: SQL injection patterns (union select, 1=1, OR 1=1), XSS patterns (<script>, onerror=, javascript:), path traversal (../), command injection (; | &&). Block, log, or flag requests. Rules in a configurable format.

title: Hungarian Algorithm (assignment problem)
desc: The Hungarian algorithm for solving the assignment problem (minimum cost matching in a bipartite graph). Given an n x n cost matrix, find the assignment that minimizes total cost. Implement the primal-dual algorithm (Munkres): subtract row/column minima, cover zeros with minimum lines, adjust uncovered elements.

title: Floyd-Warshall (all-pairs shortest path)
desc: Floyd-Warshall algorithm for all-pairs shortest path in a weighted graph. Initialize distance matrix with direct edge weights (INF for no edge), triple nested loops to relax paths through each intermediate vertex. Detect negative cycles (diagonal becomes negative). Output shortest distances for all node pairs.

title: Edmonds-Karp / Dinic Max Flow
desc: Maximum flow using Edmonds-Karp (BFS-based augmenting paths) or Dinic (level graph, blocking flow). Given a directed graph with edge capacities, find the maximum flow from source to sink. Edmonds-Karp: O(VE^2). Dinic: O(V^2E) with level graph and DFS for blocking flow. Support min-cut computation.

title: Knuth-Morris-Pratt String Search
desc: Knuth-Morris-Pratt (KMP) string search algorithm. Preprocess the pattern to compute the failure/longest-prefix-suffix (lps) array. Then scan the text: on mismatch, use lps to skip characters. Complexity O(n+m). Search for all occurrences of the pattern in the text.

title: Burrows-Wheeler Transform (BWT)
desc: Burrows-Wheeler Transform (BWT) for text transformation used in compression. BWT: create all rotations of input, sort them, take last column. Reversible via LF mapping. Apply move-to-front transform and run-length encoding after BWT. Demonstrate compression on sample text.

title: Huffman Coding Visualizer
desc: A Huffman coding visualizer. Build Huffman tree from character frequencies, assign codes (0/1), show the tree structure graphically or as indented text, encode input text, compress/decompress. Display compression ratio, frequency table, and code table.

title: L-System / Fractal Grammar Generator
desc: An L-System (Lindenmayer system) / fractal grammar generator. Define an alphabet, axiom, and production rules. Apply rules iteratively. Interpret symbols as turtle graphics commands (F=forward, +=turn left, -=turn right, [=push state, ]=pop state). Generate fractals: Koch curve, dragon curve, Sierpinski, plant branching structures.

title: Voronoi Diagram / Fortune's Algorithm
desc: Voronoi diagram using Fortune's algorithm (sweep line). Maintain a beach line (parabolas) and a priority queue of events (site and circle events). As the sweep line moves, emit edges when circle events occur. Handle degenerate cases and bounding the diagram. Output as segments or cells.

title: Delaunay Triangulation
desc: Delaunay triangulation of a set of 2D points (the dual of the Voronoi diagram). Implement Bowyer-Watson algorithm: start with a super-triangle enclosing all points, for each point find triangles whose circumcircle contains it, remove them, form new triangles. Ensure no triangle contains points in its circumcircle.

title: Barnes-Hut N-Body Approximation
desc: Barnes-Hut N-body approximation. Build an octree (3D) or quadtree (2D) of particles. For force calculation on each body, traverse the tree: if a cell's center of mass is far enough (θ criterion), approximate all particles in that cell as a single body at the center of mass. O(n log n) instead of O(n^2).

title: Strassen Matrix Multiplication
desc: Strassen matrix multiplication. Divide each n x n matrix into 4 n/2 x n/2 submatrices. Compute 7 products (instead of 8) using specific combinations of submatrices. Combine results. Recursive, O(n^2.807) vs naive O(n^3). Handle non-power-of-2 padding. Test against naive multiplication.

title: Karatsuba Multiplication
desc: Karatsuba multiplication for large integers. Split two n-digit numbers into high and low halves. Compute three products: z0 = low*low, z2 = high*high, z1 = (high+low)*(high+low) - z0 - z2. Combine: z2*B^2 + z1*B + z0. Recursive, O(n^1.585) vs O(n^2).

title: Miller-Rabin Primality Test
desc: Miller-Rabin probabilistic primality test. Write n-1 = d * 2^s. For k random bases a: compute x = a^d mod n, repeat squaring s times checking for non-trivial square roots. If all bases pass, n is likely prime. Configurable iterations for desired confidence (k=10 gives < 10^-6 error).

title: Levenshtein / Damerau-Levenshtein Distance
desc: Levenshtein distance (edit distance) between two strings: minimum single-character edits (insert, delete, substitute) to transform one into the other. Dynamic programming O(mn). Optionally implement Damerau-Levenshtein (adds transposition). Show alignment and backtrack to show edit operations.

title: Longest Common Subsequence (LCS)
desc: Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) between two sequences. DP table O(mn), fill using recurrence: if match, dp[i][j] = 1 + dp[i-1][j-1]; else dp[i][j] = max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]). Backtrack to find the actual LCS string. Find and display all LCS if multiple exist.

title: Maximum Subarray (Kadane's Algorithm)
desc: Maximum subarray sum using Kadane's algorithm. Iterate through the array, maintain current_max = max(arr[i], current_max + arr[i]) and global_max = max(global_max, current_max). O(n). Return the maximum subarray sum and optionally the subarray indices. Works for all-negative arrays.

title: Reservoir Sampling
desc: Reservoir sampling for selecting k random items from a stream of unknown length. Fill reservoir with first k items. For each subsequent item at position i (i > k), select it with probability k/i and replace a random item in the reservoir. Each item has equal probability k/n of being selected.

title: Clipboard Manager (history, categories)
desc: A clipboard manager with history and categories. Monitor clipboard changes, store recent entries (text, images, files) in a local database. Show a searchable history window on hotkey. Support favorites, categories/tags, and quick paste. Sync clipboard across devices optionally.

title: Screen Recorder (video capture)
desc: A screen recorder that captures video of the screen or a selected region. Use platform APIs (X11/Windows DXGI/macOS AVFoundation) for frame capture. Encode frames to video (H.264/VP9) using FFmpeg or similar. Support audio recording from microphone/system. Configurable FPS, quality, and region selection.

title: Screenshot Annotation Tool
desc: A screenshot annotation tool. Capture a screenshot (full screen or region), then annotate with drawing tools: rectangle, circle, arrow, line, text, highlight, blur/mosaic sensitive areas. Save annotated image to file or clipboard. Use layers for non-destructive editing.

title: Window Switcher (Alt-Tab replacement)
desc: A keyboard-driven window switcher (Alt-Tab replacement). On hotkey, show a list of open windows with thumbnails. Filter by typing. Select with arrows and Enter. Arrange by: recency, workspace, application. Use X11/Windows/macOS window management APIs for listing, focusing, and thumbnail capture.

title: Instant File Search (Everything-like)
desc: An instant file search tool like Everything. Build an index of all files/directories on the filesystem (NTFS MFT, ext4 journal, or filesystem traversal). Index: file name, path, size, modified date. Query with partial matches, wildcards, boolean operators. Show results in real-time as user types. Fast indexed search.

title: Disk Usage Analyzer (TreeMap view)
desc: A disk usage analyzer with treemap visualization. Scan a directory tree, compute size of each file/folder. Render a treemap: nested rectangles where area represents relative size. Color-code by file type or modification date. Support drill-down (click to zoom into a folder). Interactive: hover shows details.

title: Color Picker / Eyedropper (screen)
desc: A color picker / eyedropper tool that captures the color at the cursor position on screen. Click to sample and show the color in hex, RGB, HSL formats. Zoomed view of the pixel area for precise selection. History of picked colors. Crosshair cursor for accuracy.

title: Font Preview & Manager
desc: A font preview and manager. List installed system fonts with their names and styles. Show a preview of each font with sample text input (customizable). Support filtering by font type (serif, sans-serif, monospace, display, handwriting). Install/remove font files.

title: Batch File Renamer
desc: A batch file renamer. Apply rename rules to multiple files: find/replace in filename, add prefix/suffix, change extension, insert date/time, number sequentially with padding, convert case. Preview changes before applying. Undo/rollback capability. Support regex patterns.

title: Duplicate File Finder
desc: A duplicate file finder. Scan directories, compute file hashes (SHA-256) in a first pass by file size (group by size first), then compare hashes within same-size groups. Show duplicate groups with file paths, sizes, last modified dates. Option to delete, move to trash, or symlink duplicates.

title: Quick Launcher (Alfred / Spotlight-like)
desc: A quick launcher similar to Alfred or Spotlight. On hotkey, show a search bar. Index applications, files, folders, bookmarks, contacts, calculator. Fuzzy-match queries against indexed items. Execute actions: open file/URL, run command, do web search, perform calculations. Plugin system for custom workflows.

title: Pomodoro Timer + Task Tracker
desc: A Pomodoro timer with task tracking. Timer cycles: 25 min work, 5 min short break, after 4 work sessions take 15 min long break. Track tasks: add, mark complete, log time spent per task in Pomodoro units. Notifications on timer completion. Statistics: sessions/day, tasks completed.

title: Habit Tracker with Streak Counting
desc: A habit tracker with streak counting. Define habits with frequency (daily, weekly, custom schedule). Check off completed habits each day. Visualize streaks (consecutive days), completion rate, and calendar heatmap. Reminders/notifications. Support multiple habit categories and data export.

title: Flashcard App (Anki-like, spaced repetition)
desc: A flashcard app with spaced repetition (Anki-like, SM-2 algorithm). Create decks and cards (front/back, optionally with images). Review: show front, reveal back, rate recall (0-5). SM-2 calculates next review interval based on rating. Track reviews, due cards, and statistics (retention rate).

title: Recipe Manager / Meal Planner
desc: A recipe manager / meal planner. CRUD for recipes with ingredients, instructions, prep/cook time, servings, tags. Search/filter by ingredients or tags. Generate meal plans for the week with optional nutrition tracking. Generate shopping list from planned meals. Scale recipe servings.

title: Document Scanner (camera-based)
desc: A document scanner using a camera (smartphone/ webcam). Capture image, detect document edges (Canny edge detection + Hough transform or findContours), apply perspective transform to get a top-down view, enhance image (auto-contrast, sharpen), and save as PDF or JPEG.

title: E-Book Reader (EPUB/MOBI)
desc: An e-book reader for EPUB and MOBI files. Parse EPUB (ZIP container with XHTML content, OPF metadata, NCX navigation). Render HTML content with CSS styling, reflowable layout, configurable font/size, bookmark support, search within book, table of contents, and reading progress tracking.

title: Budget Tracker / Personal Finance
desc: A budget tracker / personal finance app. Track income and expenses with categories. Set monthly budgets per category, visualize spending vs budget (pie/bar charts). Support recurring transactions, transaction search/filter, bank CSV import, and net worth calculation over time.

title: Cross-Platform Calculator App
desc: A cross-platform calculator app (basic and scientific). Perform basic arithmetic (+, -, *, /, %, parentheses), scientific functions (sin, cos, tan, log, ln, sqrt, power, factorial). Build UI using cross-platform framework (Flutter, React Native, Qt, Tauri). Support history of calculations.

title: Weather App with Live Animations
desc: A weather app with live weather animations. Fetch weather data from OpenWeatherMap or WeatherAPI (temperature, humidity, wind speed, conditions). Display current conditions with animated icons (rain drops, snowflakes, sun, clouds, lightning). Show hourly/daily forecasts. Location auto-detection or manual city search.

title: Expense / Budget Tracker
desc: An expense / budget tracker app. Users input expenses with amount, category, date, and optional note. Visualize spending with charts (pie chart by category, bar chart over time). Set monthly budgets per category and alert when exceeding. Data stored locally or synced to cloud.

title: Note-Taking App (sync, markdown)
desc: A note-taking app with sync and markdown support. Rich text or markdown editing with live preview. Organize notes in notebooks/tags. Search across notes. Sync between devices using a cloud backend or file-based sync (Dropbox, Git). Support attachments, checklists, and export to PDF.

title: RSS / Feed Reader
desc: An RSS / feed reader. Subscribe to RSS/Atom feeds, fetch and parse feed XML, display articles in a timeline view with read/unread status. Organize feeds in folders/categories. Mark articles as read, star/favorite, share. Support OPML import/export. Background refresh with notifications for new articles.

title: Podcast Player App
desc: A podcast player app. Subscribe to podcasts via RSS feed URL. Fetch and list episodes with title, description, duration, publish date. Stream or download episodes for offline playback. Playback controls: play/pause, skip forward/back, speed control (1x, 1.5x, 2x). Resume from last position. Playlist queue.

title: Workout / Exercise Tracker
desc: A workout / exercise tracker. Log workouts with exercises, sets, reps, weight. Track progress over time with charts (e.g. 1RM progression). Pre-built exercise library with categories (push, pull, legs). Create custom workout routines. Rest timer between sets.

title: Meditation / Focus Timer
desc: A meditation / focus timer. Guided breathing sessions (box breathing, 4-7-8). Ambient sounds (rain, forest, ocean, white noise) using oscillator-generated noise or audio files. Session tracking: duration, streak, total minutes mediated. Daily reminder notifications.

title: QR Code Scanner & Generator
desc: A QR code scanner and generator. Scanner: use camera to scan QR codes (decode Reed-Solomon error correction). Generator: encode text/URL into a QR code image (create modules with error correction level L/M/Q/H). Support different output sizes and formats (PNG, SVG).

title: Music Player (playlists, search)
desc: A music player with playlists and search. Scan a music library directory. Display albums/artists/tracks. Create and manage playlists. Search by song, artist, album. Playback controls: play/pause, next/prev, shuffle, repeat. Support common audio formats (MP3, FLAC, OGG, WAV). Display ID3 tag metadata and album art.

title: Alarm Clock with Custom Ringtones
desc: An alarm clock with custom ringtones. Set multiple alarms by time, repeat days (weekdays/weekends/custom). Choose ringtone from device audio files or system sounds. Snooze (9 min delay) and dismiss controls. Timer and stopwatch features. Show current time with large UI.

title: Step Counter / Pedometer
desc: A step counter / pedometer app. Use the device's accelerometer to detect steps via peak detection in acceleration magnitude. Count steps in real-time, calculate distance (from step count * stride length), estimate calories burned. Show daily/weekly/monthly step totals. Goal setting and progress.

title: Drawing / Sketchpad App
desc: A drawing / sketchpad app. Draw freehand on a canvas with configurable brush size, color, and opacity. Tools: pencil, marker, highlighter, eraser. Layers support. Undo/redo. Save drawings as PNG/SVG and share. Optional pressure sensitivity for stylus input.

title: Unit Converter (comprehensive)
desc: A comprehensive unit converter. Convert between units in categories: length (m, ft, in, km, mile), mass (kg, lb, oz, stone), temperature (C, F, K), volume (L, gal, cup, fl oz), time (s, min, hr, day), speed (m/s, km/h, mph), currency (live exchange rates via API).

title: Language Learning Flashcards
desc: Language learning flashcards. Create decks for vocabulary words. Show the word in the target language with hints (image, sentence context). User types/gives answer. Track correct/incorrect streaks. Spaced repetition scheduling. Support multiple languages.

title: Shopping List / Grocery App
desc: A shopping list / grocery app. Create and manage multiple lists. Add items with quantity, category (produce, dairy, meat, etc.), and checked/unchecked state. Organize by category in the store. Share lists with family (real-time sync). Barcode scanning for quick add. Price tracking.

title: Calendar / Event Scheduler
desc: A calendar / event scheduler. View by day, week, month. Create events with title, date/time, duration, location, description, recurrence (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly). Set reminders/notifications. Color-code events by category. Import/export .ics files. Overlay multiple calendars.

title: Compass / Level Tool
desc: A compass / level tool. Compass: use magnetometer sensor to show direction (N, S, E, W) with a rotating compass rose. Level: use accelerometer to measure device tilt in X and Y axes, display a bubble level graphic showing when the surface is level.

title: Barcode Scanner (product lookup)
desc: A barcode scanner app that scans product barcodes (EAN-13, UPC-A, Code 128, QR) using the device camera. Look up product information from an API (nutrition, price, reviews). Keep a history of scanned items. Build a personal inventory from scans.

title: Habit Tracker / Streak Counter
desc: A habit tracker / streak counter app. Define habits (daily/weekly), check off completion each day, track streaks (consecutive days completed), view progress with calendar heatmap, statistics (completion percentage, longest streak), and reminders to build consistency.

title: Smart Contract (Solidity) - Simple Storage
desc: A Solidity smart contract for simple storage. Implement a contract that stores and retrieves a uint256 value. Deploy to a testnet (Sepolia/Goerli) using Hardhat or Foundry. Verify on Etherscan. Write tests in Solidity or JavaScript.

title: Decentralized Exchange (Uniswap-like)
desc: A decentralized exchange (DEX) similar to Uniswap. Implement the constant product AMM formula x*y=k. Allow users to add/remove liquidity and swap tokens. Implement slippage protection, fee collection (0.3%), and LP token minting/burning. Deploy on testnet with a pair of ERC-20 tokens.

title: NFT Minter & Marketplace
desc: An NFT minter and marketplace. Mint new NFTs (ERC-721 or ERC-1155) with metadata (image URL, name, description). Marketplace: list NFTs for sale at fixed price or auction, buy, cancel listing. Royalty support (EIP-2981). Frontend for browsing, minting, and trading.

title: DAO Governance / Voting System
desc: A DAO governance / voting system. Token-weighted voting (ERC-20 or ERC-721 based). Proposals: description, voting period, options (For/Against/Abstain). Execute proposal actions if quorum and majority met. Timelock for delayed execution. Implement using OpenZeppelin Governor.

title: Merkle Tree (proof gen + verification)
desc: A Merkle tree implementation. Given a list of data items, hash them pairwise to build the tree. Generate a Merkle root and inclusion proofs (sibling hashes on the path to the root). Verify a proof: recompute root from leaf + proof path. Use SHA-256 or Keccak-256.

title: Zero-Knowledge Proof (simple zk-SNARK circuit)
desc: A zero-knowledge proof (simple zk-SNARK circuit). Define a circuit (e.g., prove knowledge of x such that x^3 + x + 5 == 35). Use circom or similar to compile the circuit. Generate proving and verification keys. Create a proof and verify it. Understand Groth16 protocol flow.

title: SPV Light Client (Bitcoin/Ethereum)
desc: An SPV (Simplified Payment Verification) light client for Bitcoin or Ethereum. Connect to full nodes, request block headers only, verify transactions against Merkle proofs included in filtered blocks (Bloom filters or compact filters). Track balances of owned addresses without downloading the full chain.

title: HD Wallet (BIP32/39/44 mnemonic)
desc: An HD wallet implementing BIP32 (hierarchical deterministic key derivation), BIP39 (mnemonic phrase), BIP44 (multicoin hierarchy). Generate mnemonic from entropy, derive master seed with PBKDF2, create extended keys (xprv/xpub), derive child keys with CKD function. Support multiple cryptocurrencies.

title: ERC-20 Token from Scratch
desc: An ERC-20 token from scratch. Implement totalSupply, balanceOf, transfer, transferFrom, approve, allowance. Events: Transfer, Approval. Deploy on testnet with initial supply. Write tests covering transfers, approvals, edge cases (transfer to zero address, exceeding allowance).

title: Staking / Yield Farming Simulator
desc: A staking / yield farming simulator. Users stake ERC-20 tokens to earn rewards. Implement staking pool with reward rate (tokens per second), reward distribution proportional to stake share, compounding (auto-restake), and unstaking with cooldown period. Frontend showing APY and earned rewards.

title: Block Explorer Web App
desc: A block explorer web app that queries blockchain data (Ethereum, Bitcoin, or a testnet). Display latest blocks, transactions, addresses, token transfers. Use an RPC provider (Infura, Alchemy) or run a local node. Search by transaction hash, block number, or address.

title: Multi-Signature Wallet
desc: A multi-signature wallet smart contract (similar to Gnosis Safe). Requires m-of-n owners to sign before a transaction executes. Submit transaction, collect signatures off-chain, execute when threshold met. Support owner management (add/remove/replace via existing threshold).

title: Oracle Price Feed Service
desc: An oracle price feed service that brings real-world data on-chain. Fetch price data from centralized exchanges (CoinGecko, Binance API), aggregate (median/twap), and periodically push to a smart contract. Handle price deviation thresholds for updates. Use Chainlink-style architecture.

title: Layer 2 Rollup (simple state channel)
desc: A Layer 2 rollup (simple state channel). Users deposit funds into a smart contract on L1. Off-chain, two parties exchange signed state updates (off-chain transactions). When finished, submit the final signed state to L1 for settlement. Handle dispute period with challenge/response.

title: Cross-Chain Bridge Relay
desc: A cross-chain bridge relay that moves tokens/assets between two blockchains. Lock tokens on source chain, emit event, relay detects event, mint wrapped tokens on destination chain. Reverse: burn on destination, unlock on source. Implement as a smart contract pair with a relayer service.

title: CI/CD Pipeline Engine (GitHub Actions-like)
desc: A CI/CD pipeline engine similar to GitHub Actions. Define pipelines as YAML with jobs and steps. Steps can run commands, build Docker images, run tests, deploy. Implement job scheduling with dependency graph, parallel execution, workspace/artifact management, and a web UI for logs and status.

title: Terraform Provider for Custom Service
desc: A Terraform provider for a custom service. Implement the Terraform Plugin Framework or SDKv2: define resources and data sources with CRUD operations. Handle state management, plan/apply, import. Build and test against a local or mock API server.

title: Configuration Management (Ansible-like)
desc: A configuration management tool similar to Ansible. Define playbooks/tasks in YAML with modules (copy file, run command, install package, manage service, create user). Execute tasks on remote hosts via SSH. Idempotent operations. Inventory management (static or dynamic). Push-based architecture.

title: Monitoring Dashboard (Grafana-like)
desc: A monitoring dashboard like Grafana. Query time-series data from various sources (Prometheus, InfluxDB, SQL databases). Build dashboards with panels: graphs (line, bar, area), gauges, tables, heatmaps. Configurable time ranges, auto-refresh, alerting rules based on panel thresholds.

title: Log Aggregator (Loki / ELK-like)
desc: A log aggregator similar to Loki or ELK stack. Collect logs from multiple sources (file tail, syslog, HTTP). Index log entries (timestamps, labels, message), store in compressed chunks, support querying (LogQL for Loki: {job=~'.*'} |= 'error'), and visualize in a dashboard with log streaming.

title: Secret Management (Vault-like)
desc: A secret management system similar to HashiCorp Vault. Store secrets encrypted at rest. Dynamic secrets (generate credentials on-demand with TTL). Lease management, renewal, revocation. Access control policies. Audit logging. HTTP API and CLI. Auto-unseal with cloud KMS.

title: Feature Flag / Toggle Service
desc: A feature flag / toggle service. Define boolean or multivariate flags per environment (dev, staging, prod). SDK to evaluate flags in application code. Admin UI for toggling flags, gradual rollout (% of users), targeting by user attributes (cohort, region), A/B testing support.

title: Status Page / Uptime Monitor
desc: A status page / uptime monitor. Periodically check configured endpoints/services (HTTP, ping, port check). Display status of each service: operational, degraded, down. Show uptime history, response time charts, incident timeline. Generate public status page. Alert via email/Slack/webhook on downtime.

title: On-Call Schedule Manager (PagerDuty-like)
desc: An on-call schedule manager similar to PagerDuty. Define schedules (weekly rotation, custom shifts). On-call engineers get notified during their shift (email, SMS, push). Escalation policies: if primary doesn't acknowledge within timeout, escalate to secondary. Incident tracking and response time metrics.

title: Blue-Green Deployment Tool
desc: A blue-green deployment tool. Orchestrate deploying a new version (green) alongside the old (blue). Switch traffic to green after health checks pass. Rollback by switching back to blue. Support for load balancer configuration updates (reverse proxy, DNS, Kubernetes service selector).

title: Canary Deployment Controller
desc: A canary deployment controller. Gradually route a percentage of traffic to a new version. Monitor error rates, latency, and other metrics. Automatically roll back if metrics degrade. Support multiple canary steps (5%, 25%, 50%, 100%). Integrate with service mesh or ingress controller.

title: Artifact Repository (Docker Registry-like)
desc: An artifact repository similar to Docker Registry. Store and serve container images (OCI distribution spec). Push/pull images via HTTP API. Support tags, manifests (schema 2), and layer storage. Implement garbage collection for unused layers. Optionally support proxy/cache for remote registries.

title: Service Mesh Proxy Sidecar (Envoy-like)
desc: A service mesh proxy sidecar similar to Envoy. Intercept inbound and outbound traffic via iptables or TUN device. Implement L4/L7 routing, load balancing (round-robin, least-request), circuit breaking, retries, timeouts, observability (metrics, distributed tracing headers), and mutual TLS between services.

title: Chaos Engineering Tool (Chaos Monkey)
desc: A chaos engineering tool similar to Chaos Monkey. Randomly terminate instances/services in production-like environments to test resilience. Support fault injection: kill processes, block network ports, inject latency, fill disk. Schedule chaos experiments with blast radius controls. Report on system behavior during faults.

title: Auto-Scaler (K8s HPA-like)
desc: An auto-scaler similar to Kubernetes HPA. Monitor a metric (CPU, memory, custom metrics via API). Compute desired replicas: desired = ceil(current_replicas * (current_metric / target_metric)). Scale up/down with cooldown period to avoid thrashing. Support min/max replica bounds.

title: Interactive Dashboard (Tableau-like)
desc: An interactive dashboard similar to Tableau. Import data from CSV/JSON/database. Drag-and-drop to create visualizations: bar, line, scatter, pie, map, heatmap. Combine into dashboards with filters that apply across panels. Export as PDF or image. Parameter inputs for user-driven exploration.

title: Choropleth Map Generator
desc: A choropleth map generator. Load GeoJSON shape data (countries, states, counties) and bind numeric values. Color each region based on value (gradient or quantile scale). Display tooltip with region name and value on hover. Use geographic projection (Albers, Mercator) to render the map.

title: Network Graph Visualizer
desc: A network graph visualizer. Display nodes and edges in a force-directed layout (Fruchterman-Reingold or Barnes-Hut for GPU). Nodes have labels, sizes, colors. Edges have weights. Interactive: drag nodes, zoom, pan, highlight neighbors on hover. Support importing from adjacency list or edge list.

title: Sankey Diagram / Flow Chart Generator
desc: A Sankey diagram / flow chart generator. Show flows between nodes where link width is proportional to flow magnitude. Compute node positions and link paths (cubic bezier curves). Color links by source or category. Interactive: hover to see flow values. Support multi-level flows.

title: Violin / Box Plot Charting Library
desc: A violin / box plot charting library. For each data category, show the distribution with a kernel density estimate (violin) and summary statistics (box: median, quartiles, whiskers, outliers). Support horizontal/vertical orientation, customizable colors, and overlaid data points.

title: Heat Map / Correlation Matrix
desc: A heat map / correlation matrix. Given a matrix of values (e.g., feature correlations), render a grid of colored cells. Use a color gradient (diverging or sequential). Display cell values on hover or as text overlay. Hierarchical clustering (dendrogram) on rows/columns for reordering.

title: Parallel Coordinates Plot
desc: A parallel coordinates plot for multi-dimensional data. Each dimension is a vertical axis. Each data point is a polyline crossing each axis at its value. Color lines by a categorical variable. Interactive: brush on axes to filter data, reorder axes. Useful for high-dimensional visualization.

title: 3D Surface / Contour Plot Renderer
desc: A 3D surface / contour plot renderer for mathematical functions z = f(x, y). Generate a mesh grid, compute z values. Render as a 3D surface with lighting (Phong shading). Or show contour lines (level sets) on a 2D plane. Interactive: rotate, zoom, pan.

title: Animated Timeline / Gapminder Chart
desc: An animated timeline / Gapminder-style chart. Show data points (countries/regions) on a scatter plot where x and y axes are metrics (e.g., income, life expectancy), point size is population, color is continent. Animate over years with a play/pause control and year slider.

title: Word Cloud Generator
desc: A word cloud generator. Given text, tokenize words, remove stop words, count frequencies. Arrange words in a canvas where font size is proportional to frequency. Use a spiral or rectangular packing algorithm to place words without overlap. Support custom shape masks and color palettes.

title: Data Cleaning / Wrangling Pipeline
desc: A data cleaning / wrangling pipeline. Accept CSV/JSON input. Operations: remove duplicates, fill missing values (mean, median, mode, specific value), normalize/scale columns, detect and handle outliers (IQR, z-score), type conversion, column rename, filter rows, merge/join datasets. Show before/after statistics.

title: Web Scraper to Structured Dataset
desc: A web scraper that extracts structured data from websites and outputs a dataset. Given URLs and CSS selectors/XPath, extract text, attributes, tables. Handle pagination, authentication (cookies, headers), rate limiting, and JavaScript-rendered content (via headless browser). Save results as CSV/JSON.

title: Time Series Anomaly Visualizer
desc: A time series anomaly visualizer. Plot a time series with detected anomalies highlighted (red markers). Implement detection methods: moving average deviation, seasonal decomposition, Twitter's AnomalyDetection, or LSTM autoencoder reconstruction error. Adjustable sensitivity. Show anomaly score over time.

title: Alluvial / River Plot Generator
desc: An alluvial / river plot generator for visualizing changes in categorical flows over time. Similar to Sankey but emphasizes flow width consistency. Compute node positions and stream paths. Color by category or flow value. Show labels for significant flows. Interactive brushing and filtering.

title: Radar / Spider Chart Generator
